meta.name: The Matter of Britain
meta.description: Delve into Arthurian legend and evade sinister forces in your pursuit of Avalon in this 8 scenario campaign.
meta.author: Frying Tonight

cards.name: The Cave of Fruit Trees
cards.text:
Easy / Standard
[skull]: -X. X is equal to the number of unrevealed locations +1. (Max -5)
[tablet]: -2. -4 if there is an [[Arthurian]] enemy at your location.
[elder_thing]: -2. If you fail, set your current location to unrevealed.

cards.name: The Cave of Fruit Trees
cards.text:
Hard / Expert
[skull]: -X. X is equal to the number of unrevealed locations +2.
[tablet]: -3. -6 if there is an [[Arthurian]] enemy at your location.
[elder_thing]: -3. If you fail, move to any revealed location and shuffle your previous location into the Caves deck.

cards.flavor: The paths and caverns wind around each other in a seemingly impossible fashion. Within the darkness you feel the eyes of things that shouldn't possibly exist.
cards.name: Lost in the Dark
cards.text: [action] If all Caves locations are revealed, and there are no clues on those locations: Search the encounter deck and discard pile for an enemy and draw it.

cards.name: The Black Knight Rises
cards.text:
Am ominous clanking fills the caves. From one of the many paths a figure emerges; a knight clad from head to toe in black armour. Even just looking upon it fills you with a sense of dread and you instinctively know nothing you can do will be able to stop it.
Spawn the set-aside The Black Knight at the lead investigator's location. Shuffle the set aside Black Knight encounter set and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

cards.flavor: The impossible paths of the caverns mean that no matter what route you take the Black Knight is always one step behind you, giving you one more issue to consider as you look for a way to escape.
cards.name: The Hunter and the Hunted
cards.text: [action] If all Caves locations are revealed, and there are no clues on those locations: search the encounter deck and discard pile for an enemy and draw it.

cards.name: No Respite
cards.text:
The knight is relentless in it's pursuit. It is only a matter of time until you can no longer escape.
The Black Knight moves to the location with the most investigators. Each investigator at that location tests [agility](1). Resolve The Black Knight's forced effect as if it were the enemy phase, ignoring any investigators who succeeded.

cards.flavor: After a near miss you know that if you can't find some way to escape the Black Knight soon you'll meet the same fate as the expedition team you came to find.
cards.name: Impending Demise
cards.text: [action] If all Caves locations are revealed, and there are no clues on those locations: Search the encounter deck and discard pile for an enemy and draw it.

cards.name: Caught!
cards.text:
Finally exhausted, you collapse to the ground.The ominous clanking behind you grows louder and you have no choice but to brace for the inevitable.
The Black Knight makes an immediate attack that cannot be cancelled against each investigator not at Hidden Grove. Each investigator not at Hidden Grove is defeated. If at least one investigator is at Hidden Grove, advance to Act 3b. Otherwise, →R1.

cards.flavor: With the entrance sealed you have no choice but to explore the cave ahead in search of both an exit and the missing expedition team.
cards.name: One Step Forward
cards.text: [free]Spend 1 clue: Put a random location from the Caves Deck into play. Objective - At the end of the round, if there are 1[per_investigator] + 2 or more revealed Caves locations, advance.

cards.name: Two Steps Back
cards.text:
Having explored a number of the caverns you are no closer to finding an exit. Despite this, you get the feeling you are starting to gain an understanding of the seemingly unfathomable paths. With enough investigation you feel you may be able to discover the route to the heart of the caves. Whether that will have a means to escape is another matter.
Put the set aside "Tracking" rules card into play. These rules remain in effect until the end of the scenario.

cards.flavor: The only option available to you is head into the cave depths, but first you'll need to confirm the path by establishing some consistency in the caverns you've already traversed.
cards.name: We Need To Go Deeper
cards.text: [free]Spend 1 clue: Put a random location from the Caves Deck into play. Objective - At the end of the round, if there are 1[per_investigator] + 2 [[Cave]] or more locations with at least one track token on, advance.

cards.name: The Path Forward
cards.text:
If it is currently Agenda 1a, advance to Agenda 1b.
As you travel though the caverns and put together a map of the paths you notice markings on several of the walls. They look fairly recent and were most likely left by a member of the doomed expedition crew. As well as confirming your suspicions about the cave's layout, several of them refer to a location called the 'Grove'. With the Black Knight breathing down your neck you can only hope this new destination will provide you some safety.
Put the set-aside Hidden Grove location into play.

cards.flavor: With the help of the markings on the wall you know the route to the Grove but first you will need to lose both the unnatural creatures hunting you and the Black Knight.
cards.name: The Grove
cards.text: [free]Spend 1 clue: Put a random location from the Caves Deck into play. Objective - If each undefeated investigator is at Hidden Grove, advance.

cards.name: A Moment of Tranquility
cards.text:
The surprise of finding such lush greenery deep within an English mountain with no sunlight is dampened somewhat by the other unusual events of the day. As you walk through the grove a supernatural calm surrounds you and you subconsciously know that the Black Knight is unwilling, or unable, to set foot in this place. You are safe, for now.
2

cards.flavor: A makeshift camp most likely put together by the expedition team. The previous inhabitants are missing, or worse.
cards.name: Caves
cards.text: Forced - After you reveal Abandoned Camp: Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a copy of Crazed Archaeologist and draw it. Shuffle the encounter deck.

cards.flavor: A pathway leading to another section of the caves. You aren't certain if you've walked this route before, but even then you can't know if the destination will be the same.
cards.name: Caves
Caves
cards.text: Forced - At the end of the investigation phase, if there are no investigators at this location: Remove a track token from this location. If there are no track tokens on this location, set it to unrevealed. Haunted - Remove a track token from this location.

cards.flavor: A pathway leading to another section of the caves. You aren't certain if you've walked this route before, but even then you can't know if the destination will be the same.
cards.name: Caves

cards.flavor: As dangerous as it is pretty.
cards.name: Caves
cards.text: Forced - After you reveal Field of Mushrooms: Test [willpower](3). If you fail, take 1 horror. Haunted - Take 1 horror.

cards.flavor: A pathway leading to another section of the caves. You aren't certain if you've walked this route before, but even then you can't know if the destination will be the same.
cards.name: Caves
Caves
cards.text: You cannot move to Narrow Passage if there is already an investigator at this location. Forced - If there are two or more investigators at this location: Move each investigator other than the investigator with the highest [agility] to any other revealed Caves location. If there are no other revealed Caves location, put a random location from the Caves deck into play and move each other investigator there. Then, they each take 1 damage.

cards.flavor: A pathway leading to another section of the caves. You aren't certain if you've walked this route before, but even then you can't know if the destination will be the same.
cards.name: Caves
Caves
cards.text: Forced - After you reveal Steep Ravine: Test [agility](3). If you fail, move to any other revealed Caves location and take 1 damage. Haunted - Move to any other revealed Caves location and take 1 damage.

cards.flavor: A pathway leading to another section of the caves. You aren't certain if you've walked this route before, but even then you can't know if the destination will be the same.
cards.name: Caves
Caves
cards.text: Forced - When you would move to a location as a result of an effect on a scenario card or because a Caves location left play: You must choose Underground Lake as your new location.

cards.flavor: A pathway leading to another section of the caves. You aren't certain if you've walked this route before, but even then you can't know if the destination will be the same.
cards.name: Caves
Caves
cards.text: Forced - After you reveal Unstable Pathway: Lose 1 resource. If you cannot, move to any revealed Caves location and shuffle this location into the Caves deck. Haunted - Lose 2 resources. If you cannot, move to any revealed Caves location and shuffle this location into the Caves deck.

cards.flavor: A pathway leading to another section of the caves. You aren't certain if you've walked this route before, but even then you can't know if the destination will be the same.
cards.name: Caves
Hidden Grove
cards.text:
Hidden Grove cannot leave play or become unrevealed.
Enemies cannot enter Hidden Grove.
You cannot move to Hidden Grove while there are less than 3 track tokens or any ready enemies at your current location.

cards.name: Hidden Grove
cards.text:
Hidden Grove cannot leave play.
Enemies cannot enter Hidden Grove.
You cannot move to Hidden Grove while there are less than 3 track tokens or any ready enemies at your current location.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: Prey - Most clues. Hunter. Aloof. The Black Knight cannot be engaged, damaged, or evaded. The Black Knight's location gains: Haunted - Trigger The Black Knight's forced effect as if it were the enemy phase. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: The Black Knight attacks each investigator at its location, dealing either its damage or horror value.

cards.name: Afraid and Alone
cards.text: Peril. Revelation - If there is only one revealed Caves location or you are at a location with another investigator, draw and resolve the next encounter card then shuffle Afraid and Alone back into the encounter deck. Otherwise, test [willpower](3). If you fail, move to any revealed Caves location then shuffle your original location into the Caves deck.

cards.name: Fate of Those Before
cards.text: Revelation - If there are no unrevealed Caves locations, Fate of Those Before gains surge. Attach Fate of Those Before to any unrevealed Caves location. Attached location cannot have track tokens placed on it and gains Haunted - Discard Fate of Those Before. Forced - If Fate of Those Before leaves play by an effect other than the above Haunted effect: Each investigator takes 1 direct horror.

cards.name: Ominous Darkness
cards.text: Revelation - Test [intellect](2). This test gets +1 difficulty for each clue you have. If you fail, lose an action. Then, if you have one or more clues, spend a clue and put a random location from the caves deck into play.

cards.name: Raiding Party
cards.text: Revelation - Each investigator at your location chooses either [combat] or [agility]. Then, they each test their chosen skill(3). If they fail: - If they tested [combat]: They take 1 damage, plus 1 additional damage for each other investigator thatchose [agility]. - If they tested [agility]: They take 1 horror, plus 1 additional horror for each other investigator that chose [combat].

cards.name: Unfathomable Paths
cards.text: Revelation - Test [agility](X), where X is equal to the current act + 1. For each point you fail by, choose one of the following: - Lose a resource. - Remove a track token from a location. - Set a location with no investigators to unrevealed.

cards.flavor: Folklorists may not agree on what form a boggart takes, but they have also not come face to face with this beast.
cards.name: Boggart
cards.text: Prey - Lowest [willpower] Hunter. Retaliate. Forced - When Boggart enters a location with at least one track token: Remove one track token from that location.

cards.flavor: You had expected to find the bodies of the expedition team, but not like this.
cards.name: Crazed Archaeologist
cards.text: Forced - When Crazed Archaeologist is defeated: choose one (If you dealt excess damage choose twice): - Place a clue (from the token pool) on Crazed Archaeologist's location. - Discover a clue at Crazed Archaeologist's location.

cards.flavor: Tin miners say the sound of knocking is a sign of an iminent cave-in. Whether it's a warning or a cause is unknown.
cards.name: Knocker
cards.text: Spawn - Empty revealed location with fewest track tokens. Aloof. Patrol (Empty revealed location with fewest track tokens.) Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if Knocker is ready and unengaged: Shuffle Knocker's location into the Caves deck.

cards.name: Kobold Pathfinder
cards.text: Alert. Forced - When Kobold Pathfinder is defeated: Put a location from the caves deck into play. If you dealt excess damage, you may immediately move to that location.

cards.flavor: You aren't the only one with the ability to navigate these caves.
cards.name: Kobold Tracker
cards.text: Spawn - Any empty location. Forced - When a track token is placed on any location: Place 1 doom on Kobold Tracker.

cards.name: London Calling
cards.text:
Easy / Standard
[skull]: -X. X is equal to the number of [[Police]] enemies in play.
[cultist]: -2. If you fail, place 1 doom on the nearest [[Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table]] enemy.
[tablet]: -3. If Morgan Le Fey is in play, you may spend 1 resource to treat this as 0.
[elder_thing]: -3. If you fail, and the Black Knight is unengaged, it moves once towards your location.

cards.name: London Calling
cards.text:
Hard / Expert
[skull]: -X. X is equal to the number of [[Police]] enemies in play. If you fail, remember that you have "caused trouble with the police".
[cultist]: -4. If you fail, place 1 doom on the nearest [[Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table]] enemy.
[tablet]: -6. If Morgan Le Fey is in play, you may spend 2 resources to treat this as 0.
[elder_thing]: -5. If you fail, and the Black Knight is unengaged, it moves once towards your location then makes an immediate attack.

cards.flavor: The Metropolitan Police have received a tip-off from a respected organisation and are on the lookout for any Americans behaving suspiciously. Dealing with them may have consequences, but only if they can get you booked in at the station.
cards.name: The London Scene
cards.text: Each [[Police]] enemy gains [action]: Parley. Test [intellect](3) or [willpower](3). If you succeed, discard this enemy. If this enemy is [[Elite]], automatically evade it instead.

cards.name: A Familiar Figure
cards.text:
A cheer arises from a nearby crowd and you turn to see a member of the Order being launched across the street. From the crowd emerges a figure dressed in black armour, the same knight you had encountered within the caves.
Spawn the set-aside The Black Knight at the lead investigator's location. Shuffle the set aside Black Knight encounter set and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

cards.flavor: Most of the locals seem to think the chaos is some kind of St. George's Day celebration, or perhaps they just don't want to get involved in something that isn't their business.
cards.name: The Knight's Tale
cards.text: Each [[Police]] enemy gains [action]: Parley. Test [intellect](3) or [willpower](3). If you succeed, discard this enemy. If this enemy is [[Elite]], automatically evade it instead.

cards.name: The Thin Blue Line
cards.text:
Police presence continues to grow as the day goes on. "Excuse me, I'd just like to ask you a couple of questions." You find yourself face to face with one of the patrolmen.
Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a Bobby enemy and spawn it engaged with the lead investigator, place 1 doom on it. If there are 3 or 4 investigators in the game: Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a Flatfoot enemy and spawn it at New Scotland Yard. Shuffle the encounter deck.
If Inspector Harris is not in play or the victory display.
Facing increasing pressure from the higher ups, Inspector Harris grabs his coat and heads out into the streets in pursuit of the suspicious individuals.
Put the set-aside Inspector Harris enemy into play at New Scotland Yard.

cards.flavor: With an inspector on the scene interacting with the police becomes even more risky. However, unlike the Black Knight, Inspector Harris is vulnerable to more extreme solutions.
cards.name: An Inspector Calls
cards.text: Each [[Police]] enemy gains [action]: Parley. Test [intellect](3) or [willpower](3). If you succeed, discard this enemy. If this enemy is [[Elite]], automatically evade it instead. If you fail, remember you have "caused trouble with the police".

cards.name: At His Majesty's Pleasure
cards.text:
You feel a sudden sharp blow to the back of the head and lose consciousness. When you awaken you find yourself in a jail cell.
Each investigator who has not resigned is defeated, takes 1 physical trauma, and remembers they "caused trouble with the police".

cards.flavor:
As a resident of Arkham you are no stranger to professors being kidnapped by strange cults. You immediately set about to finding clues to his whereabouts before the trail goes cold.
Another consideration is the Order of the Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table were planning to raid his office after he had already been kidnapped. It would take time but perhaps you could find out why.
cards.name: The Professor
cards.text: [action] If you are at a [[Landmark]] location, spend 1 resource to ride the underground: Move. Move to any other [[Landmark]] location. You may fight enemies at that location as if you were engaged with them this turn. (Limit once per round.)

cards.name: A Choice Already Made
cards.text:
If the investigators "searched Sir Arthur's office."
The time spent searching Sir Arthur's office has caused his trail to go cold. Now your only option is to look for this supposed relic and hope that perhaps Sir Arthur is in the same place.
Take the set-aside [[Landmark]] location, flip it and put it into play next to the act deck. Read The Plan section of the card. Advance to Act 2a - "The Shape of Things to Come".
Otherwise:
The leads you've followed haven't told you where Sir Arthur is, but have pointed you to someone who might: Charles Hershaw, archivist of the Order. He was last seen ransacking Sir Arthur's office at the British Museum.
Move each investigator and enemy at Sir Arthur's Office to the British Museum and remove Sir Arthur's Office from the game. Spawn the set-aside Charles Hershaw (The Order's Friend in High Places) at the British Museum. Advance to Act 2a - "Facing Unpleasant Facts"

cards.flavor: With the newfound knowledge that the Order of the Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table was most likely after this Arthurian relic that Sir Arthur was hiding you make it your objective to recover it first. With the location known and the plan set, all that remains is to put it in motion.
cards.name: The Shape of Things to Come
cards.text: [action] If you are at a [[Landmark]] location, spend 1 resource to ride the underground: Move. Move to any other [[Landmark]] location. You may fight enemies at that location as if you were engaged with them this turn. (Limit once per round.) Objective - Execute the plan. (You will be instructed when to advance)

cards.name: It Belongs in a Museum
cards.text:
With the relic recovered all that remains is to get it to safety. Through the crowd you see several Order knights pushing their way through towards you. It looks like you won't be getting out without a fight.
Any one investigator at the relic location takes control of the set-aside Relic Crate asset. Spawn the set-aside Charles Hershaw (In Pursuit of Knowledge) enemy at a [[Landmark]] location connected to the relic location. If there are less than 1[per_investigator] Order Knights in play, search the encounter deck and discard pile for an Order Knight and spawn it at a [[Landmark]] location other than the relic location without an Order Knight until there are 1[per_investigator] in play. Advance to Act 3a: The Last Battle.

cards.flavor: Now you know who you are looking for it's time to make him talk, one way or another.
cards.name: Facing Unpleasant Facts
cards.text: [action] If you are at a [[Landmark]] location, spend 1 resource to ride the underground: Move. Move to any other [[Landmark]] location. You may fight enemies at that location as if you were engaged with them this turn. (Limit once per round.) Objective - When there are 3[per_investigator] clues on Charles Hershaw, advance.

cards.name: The Professor's Location
cards.text:
After a chase you eventually manage to corner Charles and drag the location of Sir Arthur out of him, but not before he makes a number of snide comments about how it shouldn't have been the professor you were looking for. The police have also been increasing the scope of their search following a missing person report made by the museum director. If they find him before you do it's unlikely you'll be able to speak to him any time soon, but he would also be much safer in police custody.
Randomly select one of the 6 set-aside [[Landmark]] locations. Attach the set-aside Sir Arthur asset to the in-play version of this location, then remove all 6 set-aside locations from the game.
If Inspector Harris is not in play or the victory display.
The manhunt is being led by one Inspector Harris, who is somewhat keen to speak to you as one of the last people to make contact with the missing individual.
Spawn the set-aside Inspector Harris enemy at New Scotland Yard.
Add Charles Hershaw to the victory display. Advance to Act 3a: Far From the Madding Crowd.

cards.flavor: It feels like every member of the Order in the capital is out for your blood, or at least the relic strapped to your back.
cards.name: The Last Battle
cards.text: [action] If you are at a [[Landmark]] location: Move. Spend 1 resource to ride the underground. Move to any other [[Landmark]] location. You may fight enemies at that location as if you were engaged with them this turn. (Limit once per round.) Each Order Knight enemy loses aloof and its movement restriction. Each Order Knight gains Hunter, Victory 0. and [action] Spend 1 clue: Parley. Either deal 1 damage to Order Knight or automatically evade it. Objective - If there are no [[Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table]] enemies in play, advance.

cards.name: A Moment of Quiet
cards.text:
With the last of the Order knights repelled you find somewhere to lie low from the police for a while and investigate the crate.
4

cards.flavor: You finally know Sir Arthur's location, now you need to choose whether you want to find him before the police do.
cards.name: Far from the Madding Crowd
cards.text: [action] If you are at a [[Landmark]] location, spend 1 resource to ride the underground: Move. Move to any other [[Landmark]] location. You may fight enemies at that location as if you were engaged with them this turn. (Limit once per round.) Sir Arthur's location gains [action]: Test [intellect](3) or [agility](3). If you succeed, an investigator at this location may spend 1 clue to place 1 damage token on this location. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Place 1 horror token on Sir Arthur's location for each ready [[Police]] enemy in play. Objective - When there are 3[per_investigator] damage and/or horror tokens on Sir Arthur's location, advance.

cards.name: Free Knowledge
cards.text:
If there are more damage than horror tokens on Sir Arthur's location.
You break into a nearby building and find Sir Arthur tied and gagged in a corner.
2
If there are more horror than damage tokens on Sir Arthur's location.
You watch a large number of police raid a nearby building, and after a short while they emerge with Sir Arthur in tow.
3
If there an equal amount of damage and horror tokens on Sir Arthur's location.
When you arrive at the location you can see the police nearby waiting to make their move. Whatever choice you make, you need to make it fast.
Resolve either of the other options.

cards.flavor:
“Big Ben was beginning to strike, first the warning, musical; then the hour, irrevocable.”
- Virginia Woolf, Mrs. Dalloway
cards.name: Big Ben
cards.text: [reaction] After you successfully investigate this location, if there is an even amount of doom on the agenda: discover 1 clue at this location.

cards.name: Relic Hunting (Big Ben)
cards.text:
The Plan
If you were going to hide a relic in Big Ben, naturally you'd keep it right at the top. You just need to get up there without being spotted.
While there are no ready [[Police]] enemies at Big Ben, it gains [action] Spend up to 2 clues: Test [agility](0). You get +2 skill value for each clue spent. For each point you succeed by place 1 resource token (from the supply) on this location. When there are 3[per_investigator] resource tokens on Big Ben the plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
Hidden within the machinery of the clock is a large crate marked by the British Museum, given the circumstances it could only be the supposed relic.
Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor:
“Big Ben was beginning to strike, first the warning, musical; then the hour, irrevocable.”
- Virginia Woolf, Mrs. Dalloway
cards.name: Big Ben
cards.text: [reaction] After you successfully investigate this location, if there is an odd amount of doom on the agenda: discover 1 clue at this location.

cards.name: Relic Hunting (Big Ben)
cards.text:
The Plan
If you were going to hide a relic in Big Ben, naturally you'd keep it right at the top. You just need to get up there without being spotted.

For each point you succeed by place 1 resource token (from the supply) on this location. When there are 3[per_investigator] resource tokens on Big Ben the plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
Hidden within the machinery of the clock is a large crate marked by the British Museum, given the circumstances it could only be the supposed relic.
Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor:
"Yesterday I visited the British Museum; an exceedingly tiresome affair. It quite crushes a person to see so much at once; and I wandered from hall to hall with a weary and heavy heart. The present is burdened too much with the past."
- Nathaniel Hawthorne
cards.name: British Museum
cards.text: [free] Discard a [[Relic]] asset from your hand or play area: Discover 1[per_investigator] clues (Group limit once per game).

cards.name: Relic Hunting (British Museum)
cards.text:
The Plan
If Sir Arthur was going to hide the relic in the British Museum, why wouldn't he just keep it in his office? Realising this is still entirely possible you prepare for a second round of investigation.
Add 2[per_investigator] clues to Sir Arthur's Office. [reaction] At the end of the round, if there are no clues on Sir Arthur's Office and there is an investigator at that location: The plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
You find a large crate hidden beneath the floorboards under Sir Arthur's desk. Given the circumstances this could only be the supposed relic.
Move any investigators and enemies at Sir Arthur's Office to British Museum and remove Sir Arthur's Office from the game. Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor:
"Yesterday I visited the British Museum; an exceedingly tiresome affair. It quite crushes a person to see so much at once; and I wandered from hall to hall with a weary and heavy heart. The present is burdened too much with the past."
- Nathaniel Hawthorne
cards.name: British Museum
cards.text: [free]While you are investigating this location: This location gets +2 should. If this investigate is successful, discover an additional clue. (Limit once per investigation).

cards.name: Relic Hunting (British Museum)
cards.text:
The Plan
If Sir Arthur was going to hide the relic in the British Museum, why wouldn't he just keep it in his office? Realising this is still entirely possible you prepare for a second round of investigation.
Add 2[per_investigator] clues to Sir Arthur's Office. [reaction] At the end of the round, if there are no clues on Sir Arthur's Office and there is an investigator at that location: The plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
You find a large crate hidden beneath the floorboards under Sir Arthur's desk. Given the circumstances this could only be the supposed relic.
Move any investigators and enemies at Sir Arthur's Office to British Museum and remove Sir Arthur's Office from the game. Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor: The elegant residence of King George V underwent a large amount of construction work at the start of the 20th century and managed to avoid taking any damage during the Great War.
cards.name: Buckingham Palace
cards.text: Haunted - Move to a connecting location.

cards.name: Relic Hunting (Buckingham Palace)
cards.text:
The Plan.
Breaking into Buckingham Palace is certainly a daunting prospect. Perhaps the best option here would be to cause enough incidents to attract some of the guards elsewhere.
Each location connected to Buckingham Palace gains: [reaction] After you evade an enemy: place 1 resource on Buckingham Palace. [reaction] After you defeat an enemy: place 2 resources on Buckingham Palace. [reaction] At the end of the round, if there are 3[per_investigator] resource tokens on Buckingham Palace and there is an investigator at that location: The plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
With the chaos in security you manage to slip into one of the restricted zones. In there is a large crate marked by the British Museum, given the circumstances it could only be the supposed relic.
Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor: The elegant residence of King George V underwent a large amount of construction work at the start of the 20th century and managed to avoid taking any damage during the Great War.
cards.name: Buckingham Palace
cards.text: Haunted - You cannot discover clues at this location this round.

cards.name: Relic Hunting (Buckingham Palace)
cards.text:
The Plan.

[reaction] At the end of the round, if there are 3[per_investigator] resource tokens on Buckingham Palace and there is an investigator at that location: The plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
With the chaos in security you manage to slip into one of the restricted zones. In there is a large crate marked by the British Museum, given the circumstances it could only be the supposed relic.
Advance to act 2b.

cards.name: New Scotland Yard
cards.text: You cannot move out of this location if you have "caused trouble with the police" Forced - When you successfully investigate this location: Remember you have "caused trouble with the police". [action] Parley. Spend 2 resources as bail, or 2 actions as time: You have no longer "caused trouble with the police". [action] If you have not "caused trouble with the police": Resign. You report the incident to the police and leave it to the professionals.

cards.flavor: The headquarters of the Metropolitan Police has been based on the Victoria embankment since 1890.
cards.name: New Scotland Yard

cards.flavor:
"Piccadilly, the great thoroughfare leading from the Haymarket and Regent-street westward to Hyde Park-corner, is the nearest approach to the Parisian boulevard of which London can boast."
- Charles Dickens Jr.
cards.name: Piccadilly Circus
cards.text: [reaction] After you move to this location using the Move action printed on the act: Take an evade action.

cards.name: Relic Hunting (Piccadilly Circus)
cards.text:
The Plan.
Given the area to cover and no real details in the note you have no other option than to just look anywhere remotely suspicious.
Piccadilly Circus gains [reaction] After you successfully investigate this location: Instead of discovering clues, place 1 resource token on this location. [reaction] At the end of the round, if there are 2[per_investigator] resource tokens on Piccadilly Circus and there is an investigator at that location: The plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
Behind one of the illuminated advertising hoardings is a large crate marked by the British Museum, given the circumstances it could only be the supposed relic.
Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor:
"Piccadilly, the great thoroughfare leading from the Haymarket and Regent-street westward to Hyde Park-corner, is the nearest approach to the Parisian boulevard of which London can boast."
- Charles Dickens Jr.
cards.name: Piccadilly Circus
cards.text: [reaction] After you move to this location using the Move action printed on the act: Take an investigate action.

cards.name: Relic Hunting (Piccadilly Circus)
cards.text:
The Plan.
Given the area to cover and no real details in the note you have no other option than to just look anywhere remotely suspicious.
Piccadilly Circus gains [reaction] After you successfully investigate this location: Instead of discovering clues, place 1 resource token on this location. [reaction] At the end of the round, if there are 2[per_investigator] resource tokens on Piccadilly Circus and there is an investigator at that location: The plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
Behind one of the illuminated advertising hoardings is a large crate marked by the British Museum, given the circumstances it could only be the supposed relic.
Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor: The office is a complete mess. Whatever the Order was here for it would take them, and you, a long time to find it.
cards.name: Sir Arthur's Office
cards.text: Forced - When this location enters play: Add 1[per_investigator] clues to it. Forced - When you would discover any number of clues at this location: Return that many clues at this location to the token pool instead. [free]If there are no clues on Sir Arthur's Office: Flip this card. (Limit once per game.)

cards.name: A Legendary Discovery
cards.text:
You dig through the many books and binders in the office for any kind of hint as to why the Order was so interested in the professor. Tucked away in the back of a financial report regarding the Miskatonic archaeological expedition is a hastily scrawled note. "We cannot let him find it. I have hidden the relic in the city, recover it once the exhibition is over." It would seem you have new questions for Sir Arthur, if you ever manage to find him.
Randomly select one of 6 set-aside [[Landmark]] locations. This is the relic's location. Remove the other set-aside locations from the game. Remember that you "searched Sir Arthur's Office." Flip this card.

cards.flavor:
"Three hundred years ago a prisoner condemned to the Tower of London carved on the wall of his cell this sentiment to keep up his spirits during his long imprisonment: 'It is not adversity that kills, but the impatience with which we bear adversity."
- James Keller
cards.name: Tower of London
cards.text: Tower of London gets +2 shroud for each [[Police]] enemy at this location.

cards.name: Relic Hunting (Tower of London)
cards.text:
The Plan.
Given the Crown Jewels are kept here, security is unsurprisingly high. If you want to get to the relic's most likely location, the Jewel House, you'll need to deal with them one way or another.
Spawn 1[per_investigator] copies of the set-aside Beefeater enemy at the Tower of London. [reaction] At the end of the round, if there are no Beefeaters in play and there is an investigator at the Tower of London: The plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
With the guards out of the way you have a chance to scour the Jewel House for anything that might be the Arthurian relic. In one of the back rooms you find a large crate marked by the British Museum, given the circumstances it could only be the supposed relic.
Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor:
"Three hundred years ago a prisoner condemned to the Tower of London carved on the wall of his cell this sentiment to keep up his spirits during his long imprisonment: 'It is not adversity that kills, but the impatience with which we bear adversity."
- James Keller
cards.name: Tower of London
cards.text: This location is investigated using [agility] instead of the skill indicated by the investigation attempt.

cards.name: Relic Hunting (Tower of London)
cards.text:
The Plan.
Given the Crown Jewels are kept here, security is unsurprisingly high. If you want to get to the relic's most likely location, the Jewel House, you'll need to deal with them one way or another.
Spawn 1[per_investigator] copies of the set-aside Beefeater enemy at the Tower of London. [reaction] At the end of the round, if there are no Beefeaters in play and there is an investigator at the Tower of London: The plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
With the guards out of the way you have a chance to scour the Jewel House for anything that might be the Arthurian relic. In one of the back rooms you find a large crate marked by the British Museum, given the circumstances it could only be the supposed relic.
Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor:
“Admiral Nelson, also, on a capstan of gun-metal, stands his mast-head in Trafalgar Square; and even when most obscured by that London smoke, token is yet given that a hidden hero is there; for where there is smoke, must be fire.”
- Herman Melville
cards.name: Trafalgar Square
cards.text: Haunted - Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Police]] enemy and spawn it at this location. Shuffle the encounter deck.

cards.name: Relic Hunting (Trafalgar Square)
cards.text:
The Plan.
There isn't really anywhere to hide a relic in the wide open square, so your thoughts are drawn to the sewers. As you are wondering how to get down there without unwanted attention a shady man approaches you. "Ey guv, me and me mates can help you with anything, if you grease our palms."
Trafalgar Square gains [action] Spend up to 3 resources: Place that many resources on this location. [reaction] At the end of the round, if there are 5[per_investigator] resource tokens on Trafalgar Square and there is an investigator at that location: The plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
The man and his mates stage a brawl, and while they have drawn onlookers attention you remove a nearby manhole cover and descend into the sewers. Down there you find a large crate marked by the British Museum, given the circumstances it could only be the supposed relic.
Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor:
“Admiral Nelson, also, on a capstan of gun-metal, stands his mast-head in Trafalgar Square; and even when most obscured by that London smoke, token is yet given that a hidden hero is there; for where there is smoke, must be fire.”
- Herman Melville
cards.name: Trafalgar Square
cards.text: [free]: You may discover a clue at this location. Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Police]] enemy and spawn it at this location. Shuffle the encounter deck (Limit once per round.)

cards.name: Relic Hunting (Trafalgar Square)
cards.text:
The Plan.
There isn't really anywhere to hide a relic in the wide open square, so your thoughts are drawn to the sewers. As you are wondering how to get down there without unwanted attention a shady man approaches you. "Ey guv, me and me mates can help you with anything, if you grease our palms."
Trafalgar Square gains [action] Spend up to 3 resources: Place that many resources on this location. [reaction] At the end of the round, if there are 5[per_investigator] resource tokens on Trafalgar Square and there is an investigator at that location: The plan is "complete" (resolve the text below).
Do not read until the plan is "complete."
The man and his mates stage a brawl, and while they have drawn onlookers attention you remove a nearby manhole cover and descend into the sewers. Down there you find a large crate marked by the British Museum, given the circumstances it could only be the supposed relic.
Advance to act 2b.

cards.flavor: "I must say, this is all rather thrilling, isn't it."
cards.name: Sir Arthur Bottomley
cards.subname: In for a Penny, In for a Pound
cards.text:
You get +1[intellect].
[reaction] When a [tablet] is revealed during a skill test, exhaust Sir Arthur Bottomley: If this test succeeds, gain 2 resources.

cards.flavor: Having this crate strapped to you has made you slower, thrown off your centre of balance, and made you a target for every member of the Order in London.
cards.name: Relic Crate
cards.text: You get -1[agility]. Forced - When Relic Crate leaves play: →R5

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: Prey - Most clues. Hunter. The Black Knight's location gains: Haunted - The Black Knight disengages from any investigator and readies. Forced - When you would deal damage to The Black Knight, if it is engaged: The engaged investigator automatically evades it. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase, if The Black Knight is exhausted: Ready the Black Knight, it cannot attack this phase.

cards.name: Beefeater
cards.text:
Aloof.
Beefeater cannot leave the Tower of London.
While Beefeater is engaged with you, you cannot move out of the Tower of London.
[reaction] After you successfully evade or defeat Beefeater: Remove it from the game.

cards.name: Charles Hershaw
cards.subname: The Order's Friend in High Places
cards.text: Aloof. [action]: Parley. Test [willpower](3) or [combat](3). If you succeed, an investigator at your location may place one of their clues on Charles Hershaw. Forced - When you would deal damage to Charles Hershaw: investigators at your location may place that many clues on Charles Hershaw. Forced - At the end of the investigation phase: Charles Hershaw disengages from any investigators and moves to the [[Landmark]] location furthest from all investigators.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Cultist. Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table.  Elite.

cards.flavor: "You don't know what you're dealing with."
cards.name: Charles Hershaw
cards.text: Prey - Investigator with Relic Crate only. Hunter. Retaliate. Forced - When Charles Hershaw attacks: at least 1 of the damage must be assigned to Relic Crate, if possible.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Cultist. Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Cats and Dogs
cards.text: Revelation - Put Cats and Dogs into play next to the agenda deck. Cannot be cancelled. Parley tests at each location other than Sir Arthur's Office get +1 difficulty. Forced - When you would investigate, you must decide (choose one): - Your location gets +1 shroud for this investigation. - Your location gains 'Haunted - Lose an action' for this investigation. Forced - At the end of the round: Discard Cats and Dogs.

cards.name: Heightened Security
cards.text: Surge. Revelation - Attach to any [[Landmark]] location without a Heightened Security attached. Attached location gains Haunted - Remember that you "caused trouble with the police".

cards.flavor: "You shouldn't be here, yankee."
cards.name: Local Trouble
cards.text:
You must choose either (choose one): 
- Lose 3 resources.
- Test [combat](3). For each point you fail by, take 1 damage.

cards.name: Lac Diane
cards.text:
Easy / Standard
[skull]: -1 for every two ritual tokens placed <i>(rounded up) (including those removed from the game).
[cultist]: Reveal another token. If you fail and Marigold is at your location, she immediately engages you.
[tablet]: -2. If you fail, trigger the Hunter keyword on each [[Arthurian]] enemy.
[elder_thing]: -1. Trigger Marigold's Patrol keyword. You may treat this token as -3 to ignore this effect.

cards.name: Lac Diane
cards.text:
Hard / Expert
[skull]: -1 for every ritual token placed <i>(including those removed from the game).
[cultist]: Reveal another token. If you fail and Marigold is at your location, she immediately engages you.
[tablet]: -4. If you fail, trigger the Hunter keyword on each [[Arthurian]] enemy.
[elder_thing]: -3. Trigger Marigold's Patrol keyword. You may treat this token as -5 to ignore this effect.

cards.flavor: You know I see humans get lost here all the time. They wander around for a while. Then they get tired. Then they die. You've already done the first part so how are you doing? Are you tired? I'm sure you'll be fine. I know everyone else who came here died but I don't think it'll happen to you. But don't worry. I'll keep you company even if it does. Watching the humans who come here is the most interesting thing I ever get to do. It's even more exciting than the Quiet Time Responsibility.
cards.name: Are You Tired?
He's All Shiny
cards.text:
Hey, is that a knight? I don't see many of those any more. Is he following you? Do you think he's lost too? He seems dangerous, like he'd eat me if I got too close. Ah! He's coming this way! Protect me! I don't want to be eaten!
Spawn the set-aside The Black Knight at Marigold's location. Shuffle the set-aside Black Knight encounter set and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

cards.flavor: AAH! It's going to eat me! Do something! I don't want to die like this! Who will take on the Quiet Time Responsibility? If you keep me safe I'll give you my greatest treasure! It's SUPER rare and valuable!
cards.name: Protect Me!
cards.text: The Black Knight acts after Marigold during the enemy phase. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if The Black Knight is ready and at Marigold's location: Marigold takes 1 damage. (This is considered an attack against an investigator.)

cards.name: Entertain Me!
cards.text:
"Hey. Hey. Let's do something more fun? I know! Hide and seek! Let's mix some of the forest paths up a bit too just to make it more exciting! You can race against that knight fellow!"
If Marigold is at The Lake, move her to Forest Entrance. Swap Marigold's location with the location opposite, moving all investigators, enemies and tokens, then move Marigold to the location furthest from all investigators. Trigger the Black Knight's Hunter keyword.
Evening starts to descend on the forest and while Marigold is still floating around it is clear her interest in you is starting to wane. You should look to finish your business here before night falls or your companion gets killed, or worse, bored.

cards.flavor: Okay we've been at this a while now. Let's finish this up soon or I'll have you banished from the forest so I can go take a nap. Even being eaten alive is starting to sound exciting.
cards.name: Let's Finish This Up
cards.text: The Black Knight acts after Marigold during the enemy phase. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if The Black Knight is ready and at Marigold's location: Marigold takes 1 damage. (This is considered an attack against an investigator.)

cards.name: Alright Then, We're Done Here.
cards.text:
Alright then, we're done here.
1

cards.flavor: Just leave the ritual to me! I can stay super focused, watch me! Anyways, did you know the lady always has this gruff look on her face? Maybe it's because she lives in a lake, she must be so wet all the time.
cards.name: An Adventure!
cards.text: Each [[Woods]] location other than The Lake is also connected to the location clockwise and anti-clockwise. [action] If you are at Marigold's location: Parley. Trigger Marigold's Patrol keyword. [free] If Marigold is at a non-[[Interesting]] location and unengaged, investigators at that location spend 1[per_investigator] clues, as a group: Place a resource token on that location, as a ritual. Objective - At the end of the round, if there is at least one ritual token in play, investigators may choose to advance. How does the ritual work? Spread them out, or stack them high. Either's OK. Which is better? Who could say?

cards.name: Let's Get This Ritual Started!
cards.text:
"Oh, are we ready to perform the ritual?" You're not certain why you would be the judge of that, but Marigold has already flitted away to a nearby tree.
Read Scenario Interlude: Performing the Ritual in the Campaign Guide.

cards.flavor: So for this ritual I'm going to need you to do one of the following: chant really fast, identify the birds in each tree, punch a tree or do a handstand. It is of vital importance for me... to do the ritual. Yeah. For sure.
cards.name: An Adventure! Again!
cards.text: Each [[Woods]] location other than The Lake is also connected to the location clockwise and anti-clockwise. [action] If you are at Marigold's location: Parley. Trigger Marigold's Patrol keyword. Marigold gains '[action] If Marigold is unengaged and at a non-[[Interesting]] location: Test any skill(4). Reduce the difficulty of this test by 1 for each ritual token at this location. If you succeed, place one clue (From the token pool) on The Lady of the Lake. If you succeeded by 2 or more, an investigator at your location may move one of their clues to Lady of the Lake.' Objective - If at least 3 copies of A Perfect Cut and/or Imposing Presence have been removed from the game, the investigators may choose to advance.

cards.name: Bye Bye Black Knight
cards.text:
After she finishes laughing at your latest performance Marigold floats over to another tree and once again carves a rune into it. As it starts to glow the Black Knight launches into a thunderous charge towards her. Just as its sword reaches her, it vanishes. "I've been practicing the banishment timing on wolves." she says, grinning.
6

cards.flavor: Hey, hey, how can she keep following you when she has no eyes, or ears, or eyes?
cards.name: Where's Her Head?
cards.text: Each [[Woods]] location other than The Lake is also connected to the location clockwise and anti-clockwise. [action] If you are at Marigold's location: Parley. Trigger Marigold's Patrol keyword. Excalibur cannot leave play. Objective - When the Lady of the Lake is defeated, advance. Objective - At the end of the round, if the Lady of the Lake has 1 health remaining and is at the same location as the Black Knight, advance.

cards.name: Where's Her Body?
cards.text:
If an investigator defeated the Lady of The Lake:
The lady's headless body falls to your feet and gradually disappears into the fog. The lake lies quiet and still. It would seem your original objective has ended in failure.
5
Otherwise:
You watch on as the Black Knight runs the lady's body through with it's sword. The dark fog suddenly descends and lifts just as abruptly and you find yourself alone within the forest. With the Lady of the Lake gone it would seem your original objective has ended in failure.
Add the Lady of the Lake to the victory display. Record in your campaign log that the Black Knight laid the Lady low once more. 5

cards.flavor: You never know what kind of fancy items the witches left behind. Nothing as fancy as my greatest treasure but still pretty interesting.
cards.name: Abandoned Cottage
cards.text: [action]:Test [agility](3). If you succeed, search the top 9 cards of your deck for an [[Item]] asset and add it to your hand. Shuffle your deck. If you did not succeed by 1 or more this location gains [[Interesting]] until the end of the round. Haunted - This location gains [[Interesting]] until the end of the round.

cards.flavor: You know back in the olden days certain humans apparently lived in these woods. They were witches, who I guess are like the lady of the lake? She's a sorceress though, is there a difference between a sorceress and a witch? I guess it isn't too important now, the rest of you humans solved that dilemma by killing all the witches.
cards.name: Abandoned Cottage

cards.flavor: Hey, is that Lucy? I'm going to go say hi!
cards.name: Fairy Castle
cards.text: While there is a non-[[Elite Fey]] enemy at this location, it gains [[Interesting]] and clues cannot be discovered. Haunted - Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a Fairy Guard enemy and draw it. Shuffle the encounter deck. (Limit once per round.)

cards.flavor: Hey it's my house! I'd invite you in but my mother always tells me not to bring humans back. They're dangerous, she says. They'll be the death of me, she says. I think she's just trying to hide the fact she didn't think to make the house big enough to fit human guests. When I get my own house I'll make it big enough to fit you in. If I can't then I'll make sure you at least have a chair to sit in, or...something.
cards.name: Fairy Castle
Forest Entrance
cards.text: [action]: Resign. Hey, wait! Where are you going? What about the lady of the lake? What about my entertainment?

cards.flavor: Haven't you been walking around the forest for a while now? How come you're still at the entrance? You humans aren't very good at walking in a direction. I don't know what you would have done if I hadn't found you. You better be thankful. Be sure to be entertaining for me while I help you out.
cards.name: Forest Entrance

cards.flavor: I wouldn't wander too far out without any wings. You never know how long it will take to get back on the trail and you never know when you'll run into a wolf. Or worse, a bee.
cards.name: Forgotten Trail
cards.text: If there is a Wild Wolf enemy at this location, it gains [[Interesting.]] Haunted - Lose an action.

cards.flavor: Hey, did you know you are more likely to be killed by a bee sting than by a wolf? I'm not mentioning that for any particular reason, other than the fact that there are wolves in the forest. There are probably bees here too. But I'd be more worried about the wolves. If they get you and your body isn't found, then nobody will be able to update the statistics. Maybe wolves kill more than bee stings and we just don't know it? Hey, do me a favour and find a bee and a wolf...
cards.name: Forgotten Trail

cards.flavor: Hey what are you doing? Did you eat a mushroom? How are you feeling? How many fingers am I holding up?
cards.name: Mushroom Path
cards.text: Haunted - If Marigold is at a non-[[Interesting]] location: Move Marigold once towards this location. If Marigold is at this location, she engages you.

cards.flavor: The mushroom path leads to the mushroom glade. It's where all the fairies gather for super important meetings and stuff. Not as important as the Quiet Time Duty, but still pretty important. Important enough that you can't go there. And don't even think about eating the mushrooms. Even the animals know that eating glowing mushrooms is a bad idea and you are smarter than the animals. Aren't you? Maybe you aren't. But that's why I told you. Don't eat the mushrooms.
cards.name: Mushroom Path

cards.flavor: For a mystery like this, I'd say it's worth three points. What are points? You don't need to worry about that.
cards.name: Mysterious Rock
cards.text: While there are any number of clues on and an investigator at Mysterious Rock, it gains [[Interesting]]. Forced - When there are no clues on Mysterious Rock: Replace it with the set-aside Mysterious Rock (The Truth Revealed).

cards.flavor: This is the Mysterious Rock. Named by me. Just look at it. Such a rock must hold some kind of great secret but so far I've never been able to unravel it. Lucy says its a problem with my attention span but I'm certain the rock is doing something to stop me. But now you are here. And now it is time for this mystery to be solved. Then maybe I can focus on the Quiet Time Responsibility.
cards.name: Mysterious Rock

cards.flavor: What? That's it? Nothing? Well, in that case I'm not the only one who's going to be disappointed. You know how many points this is worth? Zero. That's right. Zero. Maybe it would have been better if you left it at three points, even if you couldn't take them.
cards.name: Boring Rock
cards.subname: Sometimes A Rock is Just A Rock

cards.flavor: The moment of truth! What wondrous things will we find? Will it be even more valuable than my greatest treasure? Maybe then I can have TWO greatest treasures.
cards.name: Mysterious Rock
The Lake
cards.text: The Lake is connected to any location with a ritual token and vice versa. You cannot investigate The Lake if Marigold is not at this location. Haunted - Each other location gains [[Interesting]] until the end of the round. Immediately trigger Marigold's Patrol keyword.

cards.flavor: It's the lake! I come here occasionally on a veeery important duty to make sure the water is still as wet as it should be. My mother gave me the job, and I'm not allowed back in the house unless I've spent a couple of hours to make sure I'm super certain. She calls it the Quiet Time Responsibility. And I'm really good at it. The water has yet to be unwet on my watch.
cards.name: The Lake

cards.flavor: I don't think this river has a name. I'll call it the River Marigold. I'll charge the animals to drink from it. Do you think wolves understand the concept of capitalism?
cards.name: The River
cards.text: [free] If Marigold is at this location, spend 1 resource: remember that you paid Marigold to drink from the River Marigold.

cards.flavor: Don't be fooled! It's just a river! Not a lake! Not important enough for the Quiet Time Duty. Not important enough for there to be a Lady of the River. Some humans try to follow the river to get to the lake but the river doesn't go anywhere. I don't know how that works, magic I guess. Or maybe the river is just a big loop. But then how does water get in the river? Also magic, I guess.
cards.name: The River

cards.flavor: Are you also going to take a nap? I guarantee this is the place to do it. You'll wake up feeling completely refreshed, and possibly with a wolf gnawing at your leg.
cards.name: The Tree I Take A Nap Under Sometimes
cards.text: [reaction] At the start of the investigator phase, if all investigators are at this location: Skip the investigator phase, then each investigator heals all damage and horror. <i>(Group limit once per game).

cards.flavor: I call this tree the Tree I Take A Nap Under Sometimes. I call it that because I take a nap under it sometimes.
cards.name: The Tree I Take A Nap Under Sometimes

cards.flavor: "Each of us have a role in your play, and I suppose this is mine."
cards.name: Viviane
cards.text: [reaction] When a [tablet] is revealed during a skill test at your location, exhaust Viviane: Ignore the effect of any skill token revealed during this test. (Modifiers still apply)

cards.flavor: Her namesake flower that is about as useful as the 'Quiet Time Responsibility'. Or was it the 'Quiet Time Duty'? She was never too clear about that.
cards.name: Marigold's Greatest Treasure
cards.text:
Fast.
[reaction] When Marigold's Greatest Treasure enters play: Draw 1 card.

cards.name: Marigold
cards.text: Aloof. Patrol (If there's any [[Interesting]] locations I'm gonna go check out the nearest one. Otherwise I'll let you choose, but it has to be somewhere different.) Marigold cannot be exhausted. You cannot move while Marigold is engaged with you. [action]: Disengage Marigold from any investigators. Forced - When Marigold is defeated: Flip this card and read the text on the other side.

cards.name: A Midsummer Nightmare
cards.text:
Dark clouds swirl overhead and descend in a vision obscuring fog. You find yourself standing before the castle of the faries. A static fills the air and even the ever familiar presence of the Black Knight has gone, replaced by something perhaps even more sinister. The queen will punish those that bring harm to her daughters, all you can hope for at this point is to escape with your life.
Remove all locations, enemies, non-weakness treachery, act and agenda cards from the game. Gather the cards from the set aside Dark Forest encounter set: Take each location and put them into play. Move each investigator to the Fairy Castle. Add a copy of The Forest Closes to each investigator's threat area. Spawn the Titania enemy at the Fairy Castle. Advance to the agenda The Forest's Ruling. This card is both the current Act and the current Agenda.
If Marigold was defeated by an investigator:
You are a monster. You will not escape your sins. You will fight and die within this forest.
Remove each location other than Fairy Castle from the game.
Flip this card over over add it to the victory display.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.subname: This Scary Guy is Chasing Me!
cards.text: Prey - Marigold only. Hunter. The Black Knight cannot be engaged. Investigators at it's location may evade it as if they were engaged. The Black Knight's location gains: Haunted - The Black Knight makes an immediate attack against you, dealing either it's damage or horror value. Forced - When you would deal damage to The Black Knight: Automatically evade it.

cards.flavor: She may look a little rough but she's super skilled at magic. Plus she sometimes helps me out with the Quiet Time Duty.
cards.name: The Lady of the Lake
cards.text: Forced - When there are 1[per_investigator] or more clues on The Lady of the Lake: Remove 1[per_investigator] clues. Then, remove a copy of A Perfect Cut or Imposing Presence from next to the agenda, the encounter deck or the encounter discard from the game. Shuffle the encounter deck.

cards.name: The Lady of the Lake
cards.subname: I Like The One With a Head More
cards.text: Hunter. Prey - Investigator with Excalibur. The Lady of the Lake cannot be damaged at locations without a ritual token. [reaction] When an investigator initiates a skill test against The Lady of the Lake, spend 1 clue: The performing investigator gets +2 skill value for that test (group limit once per test). Forced - When The Lady of the Lake takes damage from any non-[[Relic]] source, reduce that damage by 1 (to a minimum of 1).

cards.flavor: Seriously though, what is that?
cards.name: Hey, What's That?
cards.text: Revelation - Test [willpower](3). If you fail attach Hey, What's That? to any location other than The Lake. If Marigold is not at your location attach it to the location with the most ritual tokens instead. This location gains [[Interesting.]] [action]: Discard Hey, What's That?

cards.flavor: You seem to have that direction covered, so I'm just going to have a quick look over here.
cards.name: Let's Go Over There
cards.text: Surge. Revelation - If Marigold is unengaged and at a non-[[Interesting]] location: Move Marigold to the connecting location furthest from all investigators.

cards.flavor: Workers have the right to one uninterrupted 20 minute rest break during their working day. Who says? Me. I say.
cards.name: Mandatory Work Break
cards.text: Revelation - Move Marigold to The Tree I Take A Nap Under Sometimes and put Mandatory Work Break into play next to the agenda deck. Cannot be cancelled. Marigold cannot move or engage by any effect. Rituals cannot be placed. Forced - At the end of the round, if Marigold did not take any damage this round: Add this card to the victory display.

cards.name: Unwanted Curiosity
cards.text: Peril. Revelation - The nearest [[Fey]] enemy engages you. If there are no [[Fey]] enemies in play, search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Fey]] enemy and draw it. Shuffle the encounter deck.

cards.flavor: If it feels like you're going in circles, it's because you are.

cards.text: Revelation - Put Whispers and Mischief into play in your threat area, While you have an even amount of total remaining health and sanity, you can only move clockwise (if possible). Otherwise, you can only move anticlockwise (if possible). [free]: Take 1 direct damage or horror. If Marigold is at your location, she engages you. (Limit once per turn) Forced - At the end of your turn, if you moved in the opposite direction to the one you could move in when your turn began: discard Whispers and Mischief.

cards.flavor: Hey is that Lucy? I don't think Lucy likes me very much after that time I tested how deep the lake was by throwing her sword into it. I don't know what she was so upset about, I heard that swords in lakes are rather popular.
cards.name: Fairy Guard

cards.flavor: My sisters and I enjoy lounging around in trees waiting for something interesting to happen. I have a dedicated tree for it, maybe you've seen it.

Easy / Standard

cards.flavor: The people within Camelot regard you with a mix of curiosity, distrust and fear. Hushed murmurs about calling the guards tells you it would be better not to linger longer than necessary.
cards.name: Stranger in the Halls
cards.text: Each location is connected to the locations to the left and right of it.

cards.name: Show of Force
cards.text:
An armed presence begins to fill into the foyer, perhaps from a barracks outside you cannot percieve. They spread and begin to sweep deeper into the castle, meaning greater caution will need to be taken where they are patrolling.
Place a horror token on the Vestibule. Shuffle the set-aside Campaign Knights enemy into the encounter deck.

cards.flavor: Security continues to spread inwards towards the depths of the castle. They may not bring your exploration of the castle to a stop immediately but they can certainly make it more difficult.
cards.name: Castle Crashers
cards.text: Each location is connected to the locations to the left and right of it.

cards.name: Security Rises
cards.text:
If all locations in play have a horror token on them:
You find yourself with nowhere left to avoid the guards and soon find yourself forcibly captured. You lose track of the time trapped in the prison but as the executioner's axe is brought down upon you, it, the executioner, and the entire castle disappear around you.
Each investigator takes 1 physical trauma. In your campaign log record that the investigators were delayed. - If it is act 1, 2, 3 or 4: →R1 - If it is act 5: 2
Otherwise:
The guards push through into the next room and quickly sweep and secure the area. It will only be a short while before they press on even deeper.
Place a horror token on the leftmost location without a horror token on it. Each investigator at a location with a horror token on it takes 1 damage or 1 horror. Flip this card back to Agenda 2a.

cards.flavor: You watch as servants hurry backwards and forwards through the foyer. When they step through the large door at the back of the room they seem to vanish before they can reach the field behind. What would happen were you to step through?
cards.name: The Foyer
cards.text: [free] Investigators spend 1[per_investigator] clues, as a group: put the top card of the Camelot deck into play to the right of the rightmost location. Objective - When there are two [[Castle]] locations in play, the investigators must immediately advance.

cards.name: Into Camelot
cards.text:
As you step through the foyer doors you realise there is no feasible way that these two rooms should connect to each other, and yet they do. The servants do not seem to consider the non-Euclidean nature of their workplace at all as they continue moving through the door behind you, as well as another door at the other side of the new room out into the field. As you take in these surreal surroundings, now more than ever you begin to wonder if this whole experience is just your descent into madness.
Each investigator must search his or her collection for a random basic [[Madness]] weakness and shuffle into their deck. This is considered added to their deck and does not count towards their total deck size.

cards.flavor: You do not know how deep this strange architecture goes, but by its nature there is only one direction it, and you, can travel.
cards.name: The Halls of the King
cards.text: [free] Investigators spend 1[per_investigator] clues, as a group: put the top card of the Camelot deck into play to the right of the rightmost location. Objective - When there are five [[Castle]] locations in play the investigators must immediately advance.

cards.name: Deeper and Deeper
cards.text:
As you have walked deeper into the castle you have noticed that while the rooms do not appear to have any pattern to them the same is not true of the people you have passed. The servants are gradually wearing fancier clothing, and the guards are becoming better equipped.
Add 1 clue to the farthest right [[Castle]] location.

cards.flavor: You feel as though you are finally approaching the end of this bizarre castle, or perhaps there is still more to be seen?
cards.name: The Endless Corridor
cards.text: Forced - When a [[Castle]] location is revealed: Add 1 clue to that location. [free] Investigators spend 1 + 1[per_investigator] clues, as a group: put the top card of the Camelot deck into play to the right of the rightmost location. [free] Put the set aside Throne Room location on top of the Camelot deck. Objective - When the Throne Room enters play, the investigators must immediately advance. (You will not be able to explore Camelot any further.)

cards.name: Before the Throne
cards.text:
As you look across the room to the next door, the knights are stood to attention in elaborate military uniform. There is only one location you know of that would require such presentation: the throne room.
If the players have not yet completed The Queen of Air and Darkness Advance to act 4a - "The Heart of the Castle (v. I)"
If the players have completed The Queen of Air and Darkness Advance to act 4a - "The Heart of the Castle (v. II)"

cards.flavor: Only one room remains. What awaits you at the very centre of Camelot?
cards.name: The Heart of the Castle (v. I)
cards.text: Objective - At the start of the investigation phase, if each investigator is at the Throne Room, advance.

cards.name: The Knight Arrives
cards.text:
"To arms! To arms! Protect the queen!" You briefly think that your presence in the throne room has been noted, but the handful of well armed knights advance past you and out towards the corridor. Looking down the corridor to the foyer the familiar sight of a figure clad in black amour fills you with dread. But then, you consider. So far you have been unable to harm the Black Knight, but here you are surrounded by knights most likely formed by whatever mysterious force summoned your pursuer. And if that is the case, perhaps they can do what you cannot.
Gather the set-aside [[Of the Round Table]] assets, shuffle them and put one into play at each location other than the Throne Room. Remove the remaining assets from the game. Spawn the set-aside Black Knight at the Vestibule. Put the set-aside The Best Defence treachery card into play next to the act deck. Shuffle the set-aside Black Knight encounter set and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck. In your campaign log, record that "The Black Knight fought against King Arthur's forces." Advance to Act 5a: Save the Queen.

cards.flavor: Only one room remains. What awaits you at the very centre of Camelot?
cards.name: The Heart of the Castle (v. II)
cards.text: Objective - At the start of the investigation phase, if each investigator is at the Throne Room, advance.

cards.name: The Knight Arrives
cards.text:
A raucous cheer erupts from back down the corridor, but that is not the sound that has caught your attention. The familiar clanking of armour fills your heart with dread. A number of knights rally around it and advance into the castle, determined to not let a mysterious intruder ruin it's triumphant return.
If Pellinore is not listed under 'Knights Slain' in the campaign log, spawn it at the Vestibule. Gather the set-aside [[Of the Round Table]] enemies, shuffle them and put one into play at each location without an [[Elite]] enemy other than the Throne Room. Remove the remaining enemies from the game. Spawn the set-aside Black Knight at the Vestibule. Shuffle the set-aside Black Knight encounter set and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck. Advance to Act 5a: A Triumphant Return

cards.flavor: With the knights of the round table advancing upon you the only thing remaining for you here is the pointy end of a sword. Looking toward the Black Knight it almost seems as though its focus isn't you, but then what is it trying to achieve?
cards.name: A Triumphant Return
cards.text: You cannot leave a location with a ready [[Of the Round Table]] enemy. [[Of the Round Table]] enemies activate their Hunter keyword in order of nearest to an investigator, and cannot move to a location with an [[Of the Round Table]] enemy. Objective - If each undefeated investigator has resigned, advance.

cards.name: Camelot Left Behind
cards.text:
You stumble out through the foyer and turn back towards your pursuers, but you are met with nothing but the empty field that you saw when you first arrived. The only evidence you have that anything was ever here is your still pounding heart and the fear instilled by the Black Knight. The knight in question doesn't appear to chasing you, as if it's goal here was something else entirely. But you have missed your chance to ever find out what it was.
2

cards.flavor: The knights of the Round Table have rallied in defence of their queen. You can't be certain what impact the death of a number of notable Arthurian figures will have in the long term, but perhaps something can be learnt by watching them fight against the black knight.
cards.name: Save the Queen
cards.text: Forced - When the Black Knight would move: - The [[Of The Round Table]] asset at it's location deals 1 damage to it. If that asset has a horror token it deals 1 additional damage. - It deals its combined damage and horror values as damage to the [[Of The Round Table]] asset at it's location. Investigators may remove a horror token from that asset to ignore the effects of each copy of A Perfect Cut and Imposing Presence for this attack. If the asset is still in play, cancel the move. Objective - When the Black Knight would be defeated, advance.

cards.name: A Knight Defeated
cards.text:
The Black Knight falls to it's knees, and the form of Camelot seems to dissolve around you as if in sync. The cheers of the remaining knights come to an abrupt stop as you find yourself alone in an empty field.
3

cards.flavor: This entry hall is filled with all sorts of peasants, merchants, knights and servants. Even with such a range of people you still look out of place.
