cards.text: The Best Defence cannot leave play. [action]: The Black Knight moves once towards its prey. If that movement was not cancelled, each investigator at that location immediately resolves its haunted effect. An investigator at any location may activate this ability. Forced - When The Black Knight is defeated, if there are no [[Of the Round Table]] assets in the victory display: Flip this card.

cards.name: A Storied Victory
cards.text:
The Black Knight has fallen, and all of the knights of the round table live to tell the tale. Perhaps you will encounter these individuals again in your own tale, but whether that will be once again as allies or instead as foes is a story for another day.
Each investigator earns an additional 5 experience. Each investigator may heal 1 mental trauma. If there are no cards in the Camelot deck, each investigator earns an additional 2 experience.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: Prey - Guinevere only. Hunter. The Black Knight gets +1[per_investigator] health. The Black Knight cannot be engaged, evaded, attacked or damaged by investigators. The Black Knight's location gains: Haunted - The Black Knight makes an immediate attack against you, dealing either it's damage or horror value.

cards.flavor: You see, we're on a mission from God.
cards.name: Divine Mandate
cards.text: Revelation - Test [willpower](3). If you succeed and there is a horror token on your location, return the revealed chaos token to the bag and reveal a new chaos token (Limit once per test). If you fail, take 2 horror, at least 1 of which must be assigned to [[Ally]] assets if able.

cards.flavor: May god have mercy on your soul, as your executioner certainly won't.
cards.name: Fitting Punishment
cards.text: If there is a horror token on your location, Fitting Punishment gains Peril. Revelation - Test [agility](3). If you fail, take 2 damage, at least 1 of which must be assigned to [[Ally]] assets if able.

cards.flavor: Even when he isn't present the king's influence can be felt throughout the court.
cards.name: Royal Decree
cards.text: If there is a horror token on your location, Royal Decree gains Surge. Revelation - Put Royal Decree into play in your threat area. You must spend 1 resource to perform move or investigate actions. [action][action]: Discard Royal Decree

cards.flavor: Did you hear what she was doing with the knight-errant?
cards.name: Rumours at Court
cards.text: Revelation - You must decide (choose one): - If there are less than three copies of Rumours at Court in play next to the agenda deck: Put Rumours at Court into play next to the agenda deck, then gain 1 resource for each copy next to the agenda deck. Rumours at Court gains surge. - If there is at least one copy of Rumours at Court next to the agenda deck: Resolve your location's haunted effect once for each copy, then discard them.

cards.flavor: "In unity we will overcome"
cards.name: Unified Decree
cards.text: If there is a horror token on your location, Unified Decree gains Surge. Revelation - Put Unified Decree into play in your threat area. You must spend 1 resource to perform fight or evade actions. [action][action]: Discard Unified Decree

cards.name: Knight of the Round Table
cards.text: If there is a horror token on Knight of the Round Table's location it gains Alert.

cards.name: Knight of the Round Table
cards.text: If there is a horror token on Knight of the Round Table's location it gains Retaliate.

cards.name: The Queen of Air and Darkness
cards.text:
Easy / Standard
[skull]: -1 for each Aloof enemy in play (Max -5).
[cultist]: -2. If you fail, move an in-play copy of Close-Knit Community to your location.
[tablet]: -3. If you fail, increase the difficulty of the next test on or against an [[Arthurian]] card this round by 1.
[elder_thing]: -3. If you fail, move an [[Arthurian]] enemy to a connecting location.

cards.name: The Queen of Air and Darkness
cards.text:
Hard / Expert
[skull]: -1 for each Aloof enemy in play.
[cultist]: -4. If you fail, move an in-play copy of Close-Knit Community to your location.
[tablet]: -5. If you fail, increase the difficulty of the next test on or against an [[Arthurian]] card this round by 2.
[elder_thing]: -5. Move an [[Arthurian]] enemy to a connecting location.

cards.flavor: Now that you're stood on the island bracing against the cold wind you start to doubt whether this trip was worth it at all. And with dark clouds in the distance you probably don't have long before you get even more doubts.
cards.name: Island Getaway
Storm Overhead
cards.text:
If the players have not yet completed A Most Magnificent Court
The storm clouds are now directly overhead, you know you don't have long before things take a turn for the wetter.
If the players have completed A Most Magnificent Court
Heavy raindrops start to strike your face. Your island exploration is about to get a lot more laborious.
Shuffle each set-aside copy of Inclement Weather and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck. Remove Agenda 2 from the game (Advance straight to Agenda 3a.)

cards.flavor: A drop in pressure and temperature are telltale signs of an incoming thunderstorm. You don't have much longer to investigate in the dry.
cards.name: Calm Before The Storm
The Storm Arrives
cards.text:
Heavy raindrops start to strike your face. Your island exploration is about to get a lot more laborious.
Shuffle each set-aside copy of Inclement Weather and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

cards.flavor: Heavy rain and mud makes exploring the island a much less pleasurable experience. There's no sign of it letting up, and if things get much worse it's possible it will have some impact on your return trip.
cards.name: How's The Weather?
cards.text: Note: If you spend much longer on the island, you may be stuck here until the storm passes.

cards.name: Rising Storm
cards.text:
You didn't think it was possible for the weather to get any worse. Clearly you haven't been on the British Isles long enough. With things this rocky it's unlikely you'll be taking a boat back to the mainland for a while.
In your campaign log record that the investigators were delayed.

cards.flavor: Only reckless American tourists would be outside in this weather.
cards.name: The Tempest
cards.text: Each copy of Inclement Weather gains Surge.

cards.name: Tidal Surge
cards.text:
You can no longer see or hear anything.
1

cards.flavor: With no particular leads to go on there's little more you can do than to explore the islands and see what clues about king Lot's castle you can gather. From your experiences so far it is probably only a matter of time until you find something or, perhaps more likely, something finds you.
cards.name: Seek, And Ye Shall Find
A Constructive Solution
cards.text:
Having explored the mainland there is little to guide you towards a 6th century castle. Many of the sites you've visited are either neolithic or medieval and while there is evidence of supernatural activities there has been nothing decidedly Arthurian. You think back on your discussions with Sir Arthur, then even further back to the ones with Morgan. She had described your experience as 'forming your own legend' and perhaps it is time to take that more literally. If you can't find the correct castle, why not just make one you do know about the correct one?
Put the set-aside Cubby Roo Gates (Ruins), Cubby Roo Chapel and Cubby Roo Courtyard into play.
If the players have not yet completed A Most Magnificent Court Advance to Act 2a: Any Castle in a Storm (v.I)
If the players have completed A Most Magnificent Court Advance to Act 2a: Any Castle in a Storm (v.II)

cards.flavor: On the nearby isle of Wyre lay the ruins of Cubbie Roo's Castle. Built in the 12th century it is obviously not somewhere you would expect to find any Arthurian activity, but if you approach it with enough confidence perhaps you can convince whatever powers are at work here otherwise.
cards.name: Any Castle in a Storm (v. I)
cards.text: Objective - At the end of the round, if each investigator is at Cubby Roo Courtyard they may spend the requisite number of clues to advance.

cards.name: Morgause
cards.text:
A lone woman in black sits amongst the rubble of what once would have been Cubby Roo's banquet hall. 'So,' she says without looking up, 'You must be my sister's newest knights. Here in pursuit of knowledge of Mordred, I'm sure. But you're not our only visitor, and I'm afraid my husband is far too busy to deal with you.' A blinding flash of light finds you standing back within the town of Kirkwall. Knights march through the town from the harbour, with a man on horseback shouting commands from the rear. 'Forward, men! For King Arthur! Follow our commander to victory!" If this man isn't the commander you have a sinking feeling you know what is.
Spawn the set-aside The Black Knight enemy at Cubby Roo Gates. Then, spawn set-aside Aspirant Knight enemies at Cubby Roo Gates until there are 1[per_investigator] [[Knight]] enemies. Spawn the set-aside Pellinore enemy at Orkney Harbours. Put the set-aside Morgause enemy into play next to the agenda deck. Spawn King Lot at Cubby Roo Courtyard. Move each investigator to Kirkwall. Replace Cubby Roo Courtyard with the set-aside Cubby Roo Courtyard (Under New Management). Shuffle the set-aside Knights encounter set, The Black Knight encounter set, and each remaining Aspirant Knight into the encounter deck. Advance to Act 3a: The Second Battle of Terrabil.

cards.flavor: On the nearby isle of Wyre lay the ruins of Cubbie Roo's Castle. Built in the 12th century it is obviously not somewhere you would expect to find any Arthurian activity, but if you approach it with enough confidence perhaps you can convince whatever powers are at work here otherwise.
cards.name: Any Castle in a Storm (v. II)
cards.text: Objective - At the end of the round, if each investigator is at Cubby Roo Courtyard they may spend the requisite number of clues to advance.

cards.name: Morgause
cards.text:
A lone woman in black sits amongst the rubble of what once would have been Cubby Roo's banquet hall. 'So,' she says without looking up, 'You must be my sister's newest knights. Here in pursuit of knowledge of Mordred, I'm sure. Ignorant of the blight you have brought upon these shores. Perhaps if we survive this crisis you have wrought you may get some answers you seek, though you may find my husband less than forthcoming.' The nearby sound of clashing steel is accompanied by a blinding flash of light that finds you standing back within the quiet town of Kirkwall. The woman may not have specified what crisis now faced the king of Orkney, but you already know.
Spawn the set-aside The Black Knight enemy at Cubby Roo Gates. Then, spawn set-aside Aspirant Knight enemies at Cubby Roo Gates until there are 1[per_investigator] [[Knight]] enemies. Put the set-aside Morgause enemy into play next to the agenda deck. Move each investigator to Kirkwall. Replace Cubby Roo Courtyard with the set-aside Cubby Roo Courtyard (Under New Management). Shuffle the set-aside Knights encounter set, The Black Knight encounter set, and each remaining Aspirant Knight into the encounter deck. Put the King Lot story asset into play at Cubby Roo Courtyard. In your campaign log, record that the Black Knight fought against King Lot's forces. Advance to Act 3a: Throw in Your Lot.

cards.flavor: With the arrival of King Arthur's forces you are no longer the only one interested in meeting King Lot, and he is probably no longer in any mind to parley.
cards.name: The Second Battle of Terrabil
cards.text: Each non-[[Elite Knight]] enemy loses Hunter and gains 'Spawn - Kirkwall and Patrol (Cubby Roo Gates).' [reaction] When a [skull] or [cultist] is revealed from the chaos bag, spend 2 resources: Treat that token as a [tablet] instead. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: For each ready [[Knight]] enemy other than King Lot at a [[Wyre]] location, place 1 horror token on King Lot. Objective - When King Lot is defeated, advance.

cards.name: Lot Defeated
cards.text:
If there is equal or more damage than horror on King Lot:
You manage to fight through both the Arthurian and Orcadian knights and take down the king. With it's prey already cornered the Black Knight and its forces vanish as quickly as they appeared.
3
If there is more horror than damage on King Lot:
Despite your best efforts it is the Arthurian knights who break through King Lot's defensive line, and you are left with no choice but to look on.
4

cards.flavor: King Lot's unknown assailant raises a seemingly endless army of knights right at the castle gates.
cards.name: Throw in Your Lot
cards.text: Each non-[[Elite Knight]] enemy loses Hunter and gains 'Spawn - Cubby Roo Gates'. [reaction] When a [skull] or [cultist] is revealed from the chaos bag, spend 2 resources: Treat that token as a [tablet] instead. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: For each ready [[Knight]] enemy at a [[Wyre]] location, place 1 horror token on this act. Objective - When there is 3[per_investigator] damage on this act: The investigators must immediately advance. Objective - When there is 4[per_investigator] horror on this act: The investigators must immediately advance.

cards.name: The Siege, Broken
cards.text:
If the act advanced due to damage:
Rallying around their king the Orcadian knights continue to repel the invaders. With your support it seems they finally have the upper hand.
5
If the act advanced due to horror:
The visions of the gates crumble as the Black Knight's forces flow unabated into the courtyard. There is nothing you can do at this point but watch the Orcadian knights be overwhelmed.
4

cards.flavor: It is all too easy to misjudge the timing of the tide and become stranded.
cards.name: Brough of Birsay
cards.text: Haunted - Your next action this round cannot be a Move action.

cards.flavor: Only accessible on foot for two hours via causeway during low tide, this tidal island houses a treasure trove of archeological remains.
cards.name: Brough of Birsay

cards.flavor: The town's inhabitants eye you suspiciously as you go about your business.
cards.name: Kirkwall
cards.text: [[Humanoid]] enemies at this location gain Aloof. Haunted - You cannot discover clues from Kirkwall until the end of the round.

cards.flavor: The only town on the island, two third of Orkney's population lives within Kirkwall. The influence of the town's Viking founders can be found throughout its architecture.
cards.name: Kirkwall

cards.flavor: The harbour is full of fishermen organising their catch.
cards.name: Orkney Harbours
cards.text: [action]: Resign. You lie low at the harbour and wait for your ferry to return. Haunted - You cannot resign this round.

cards.flavor: The largest port on the island provides both cargo and passenger connections to Leith and Aberdeen.
cards.name: Orkney Harbours

cards.flavor: Unsuprisingly, it's full of pigeons.
cards.name: Rendall Doocot
cards.text: Haunted - Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a Pigeon enemy and draw it, ignoring Surge. Shuffle the encounter deck.

cards.flavor: The Rendall Doocot was constructed in the mid 1600s to house pigeons used to feed the residents of the nearby Rendall Hall. It's circular construction sets it apart from the traditional rectangular or square style.
cards.name: Rendall Doocot
Ring of Brogdar
cards.text: [reaction] When a clue is discovered from Ring of Brogdar: Play a [[Spell]] or [[Ritual]] asset from your hand (paying its cost) (Group limit once per round). Haunted - Remove a charge from a [[Spell]] or [[Ritual]] belonging to an investigator at your location.

cards.flavor: Nobody is certain what the ring from 2500BC was used for. Some suggest it was a place of worship, others that it was a ritual site.
cards.name: Ring of Brogdar

cards.flavor: A handful of archaelogists and academics have braved the weather in pursuit of knowledge.
cards.name: Skara Brae
cards.text: [reaction] After you successfully investigate Skara Brae: Gain 1 resource. Haunted - Exhaust a [[Relic]] or [[Wayfarer]] asset.

cards.flavor: Uncovered by a storm in 1850 this Neolithic village laid hidden for over 5000 years. There is still much to be uncovered and further archaeological digs are planned for the future.
cards.name: Skara Brae
Cubby Roo Chapel
cards.text: [action]: Move. Test [agility](2). You may increase the difficulty of this test by 2. If you succeed, move to Cubby Roo Courtyard. If you increased the difficulty, an additional investigator at this location may move with you. [action][action]: Move. Move to Cubby Roo Courtyard. Haunted - Actions printed on this location cannot be performed this round.

cards.flavor: The chapel of St. Mary's was constructed by the lord of Cubby Roo's Castle a short time after the castle in the 1100s.
cards.name: Cubby Roo Chapel

cards.flavor: The courtyard may be utterly silent, but you can't shake the feeling you aren't alone.
cards.name: Cubby Roo Courtyard
cards.text: Each [skull] revealed at this location is treated as a [tablet]. Haunted - Move to Cubby Roo Gates.

cards.flavor: An eerie silence resides over the courtyard. It is clearly not the weather for tourism.
cards.name: Cubby Roo Courtyard

cards.flavor: For a fleeting moment the castle walls stand tall and knights clash amidst the flames.
cards.name: Cubby Roo Gates
cards.text: Haunted - A [[Knight]] enemy at this location attacks you. Forced - At the end of the round: Each investigator at this location takes 1 damage and may take an immediate Fight action. Flip this card (Do not resolve the forced effect on the other side this round).

cards.flavor: The castle was built in the 1150s by the Norse chieftain Kolbein Hruga, whose name was later corrupted to Cubby Roo.
cards.name: Cubby Roo Gates
cards.text: Forced - At the end of the round, if it is Act 3: Flip this card. (Do not resolve the forced effect on the other side this round).

cards.name: King Lot Courtyard
cards.text: [action]: Engage. Engage a non-[[Elite]] enemy at Cubby Roo Gates (limit once per round). [action] Investigators at this location spend any number of clues, as a group: Parley. Reveal X chaos tokens, where X is the number of clues spent. Reveal an additional token for any [bless] or [curse] revealed this way. For each [tablet] revealed, deal 1 damage to an enemy at a [[Wyre]] location. Add clues to King Lot Courtyard until there are 2[per_investigator] clues. Haunted - Move to Cubby Roo Gates.

cards.flavor: The previous silence has been broken by the rallying cries of the Orcadian knights.
cards.name: Cubby Roo Courtyard
King Lot
cards.text: [action]: Parley. Test [intellect](3). If you succeed, move a [[Knight]] enemy at Cubby Roo Gates to King Lot's location. If a [tablet] was revealed during this skill test, deal 1 damage to an enemy at King Lot's location. Forced - When a [[Knight]] enemy at King Lot's location is defeated: Place 1 damage on the act.

cards.name: King Lot
cards.text: Prey - The Black Knight only. King Lot cannot move, be engaged, or exhausted. Horror on King Lot is considered damage. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Each investigator at King Lot's location may move to a connecting location. Then, King Lot attacks each investigator at his location.

cards.flavor: "And who are you, to change the script of this 800 year perfomance?"
cards.name: Curse of Morgause
cards.text: Revelation - Put Curse of Morgause into play in your threat area. Forced - When you reveal a [tablet] token during a skill test: Treat that token as a [auto_fail]. Then, discard Curse of Morgause.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: Massive. Hunter. Prey - King Lot only. The Black Knight does not exhaust, and cannot be evaded, attacked or damaged by investigators. Forced - When the Black Knight would move: Instead, discard cards from the Encounter Deck until a [[Knight]] enemy is discarded and draw it. The Black Knight's location gains: Haunted - The Black Knight makes an immediate attack against you, dealing either it's damage or horror value.

cards.flavor: There is no shortage of young squires who seek the same glories as their masters.
cards.name: Aspirant Knight
cards.text: If this card was not drawn by the Black Knight's effect it gains Surge.

cards.name: Morgause
cards.text: Aloof. [action]: Reveal 3 tokens from the chaos bag (6 if Morgan Le Fey or Viviane is at your location). Reveal an additional token for any [bless] or [curse] revealed this way. If a [tablet] is revealed, move Morgause to your location. Any investigator at a non-[[Wyre Orkney]] location may trigger this ability. Forced - After Morgause takes any amount of damage or would change location: Move her next to the Agenda deck. Forced - When Morgause is defeated: Flip this card and resolve the text on the other side.

cards.name: An Alternative Truth
cards.text:
"Why are you even here? A fool's castle would have been enough for most. Did you think that was my work? Of course it wasn't, I'm no sorceress. At least not by any conventional telling. And yet, here we are." Up until now you had been labouring on the principal that you operated within what had been told before. But what if that's not the case? Perhaps the phrase 'your own legend' could be taken more literally.
If the players have not yet completed A Most Marvelous Court: Deal 3[per_investigator] damage to King Lot. If he would be defeated by this effect, instead deal damage until he has 1 health remaining. Add an Alternative Truth to the victory display.
Otherwise: Place 1[per_investigator] horror on the act. This can cause the act to advance. Then, place 2[per_investigator] damage on the act. Add an Alternative Truth to the victory display.

cards.flavor: “Now you are in my power, to slay or spare as I will! And I will kill you forewith, unless you kneel and yield to me, confessing yourself to be a knight of little worth.”
cards.name: Pellinore
cards.text:
Aloof. Retaliate.
You cannot move while Pellinore is engaged with you.
You cannot resign while Pellinore is at your location.

cards.name: Gaze of the Selkie
cards.text: Revelation - Test [willpower](3). If you fail, put Gaze of the Selkie into your threat area. Forced - After you move to a location: each non-[[Elite]] aloof enemy at that location engages you. If at least one enemy engaged you through this effect, discard Gaze of the Selkie.

cards.flavor: Whether you try to escape the storm or stand and brace it, neither will be a fun experience.
cards.name: Inclement Weather
cards.text: Revelation - You must decide (Choose one.): - Test [agility](3). If you fail, you cannot take move actions this round. - Test [combat](3). If you fail, the next skill test you perform this round has +2 difficulty.

cards.flavor: Known on both sides of the pond as flying rats.
cards.name: Pigeon
cards.text: Surge. Spawn - Rendall Doocot Aloof. Forced - When Pigeon enters play: Place 1 clue (from the token bank) on Rendall Doocot. Clues cannot be discovered at Pigeon's location.

cards.name: Trow
cards.text: Spawn - Any adjacent location. Aloof. Each location adjacent to Trow's location gains: Haunted - Move to Trow's location. Then, Trow engages you. Forced - When Trow engages you: You cannot move this round.

cards.name: Prophetiae Merlini
cards.text:
Easy / Standard
[skull]: -1 for each story card in the victory display (Max -5).
[cultist]: -2. If you fail, place 1 doom on the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy.
[tablet]: 0. Reveal another token. If you fail and this test was on or against an [[Arthurian]] card, place 1 doom on that card.
[elder_thing]: -2. If this test was printed on a story card, -5.

cards.name: Prophetiae Merlini
cards.text:
Hard / Expert
[skull]: -1 for each story card in the victory display.
[cultist]: -4. If you fail, place 1 doom on the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy.
[tablet]: -2. Reveal another token. If you fail and this test was on or against an [[Arthurian]] card, place 1 doom on that card.
[elder_thing]: -5. If you fail and this test was printed on a story card, lose an action.

cards.flavor: It's still early days for the exhibition and as such attendance is still at a high. All around you is the hubbub of people having a good time. You can only hope you can keep it that way.
cards.name: The Sound of the Crowds
A Familiar Face in the Crowd
cards.text:
You can feel it. You can hear it. You knew it would come here after you, and so it has.
Spawn the set aside The Black Knight enemy at the location furthest from all investigators. Shuffle the set aside Black Knight encounter set into the encounter deck.

cards.flavor: No matter where you are in the exhibition you can hear it. Through the boisterous clamour of the crowd. Through the shouts of the cultists you encounter. You can hear it.
cards.name: The Sound of Clanking Armour
The Inspector on Overtime
cards.text:
A furor rises from the entrance on the arrival of numerous men in the uniform of the metropolitan police. Led by Inspector Harris, they're here to put a swift end to the disorder. And that includes you.
Replace Entrance Gate with the set-aside Police Checkpoint. If The Fortune Teller story card is in play, remove it from the game. Spawn the set-aside Inspector Harris enemy at Police Checkpoint. Check campaign log. If the investigators injured Inspector Harris in London, deal 1 damage to Inspector Harris. Shuffle the set-aside Metropolitan Police encounter set and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.
Check Campaign Log. If Sir Arthur was rescued by the police:
The police move to arrest several members of the Order. It looks like they're acting on a tip-off from Sir Arthur.
For the remainder of the game the agenda gains: [reaction] When doom would be placed on a [[Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table]] enemy, if there is a [[Police]] enemy at it's location: Cancel that doom. (Group limit 1[per_investigator] per round.) (Keep this card next to the agenda as a reminder).

cards.flavor: An increase in uniformed presence has had little impact on the crowd's spirits, but the officers themselves are tense and on high alert. It is only a matter of time before that has an impact.
cards.name: The Sound of The Police
The Crowd Moves
cards.text:
'The feeling that something is wrong' has begun to spread throughout the guests at the exhibition. Your actions draw judgemental looks and hushed whispers, further heightening your paranoia.
Each investigator in turn searches the encounter deck and discard pile for a copy of Eyes Everywhere and draws it. Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

cards.flavor: A tense energy fills the air, guests glance around nervously or briskly make their ways towards the exits. Nobody will openly acknowledge it but the current tension is a powder keg waiting to explode.
cards.name: The Sound of Disquiet
Closing Time
cards.text:
Despite everything that's happened, the day closes on another outwardly peaceful day of the British Empire Exhibition. Ushers are assisted by the police in escorting guests to the exit and you are no exception. Each guest is scrutinised as they pass through the police checkpoint. This may be an issue for some of you.
Each investigator with a [[Weapon]] asset in play remembers that they 'caused trouble with the police.' Each investigator that has not resigned is defeated.

cards.flavor: Hidden away within their pavilion the cultists of the order work towards their ends. Note: The ritual will progress regardless of your efforts. Perhaps your time would be better focused on gathering information rather than trying to slow the cultists down.
cards.name: The Order at Work
cards.text: Doom cannot be placed on this agenda during the 'place doom' step of the mythos phase. Doom on this agenda does not count towards the doom threshold of agenda a and is not removed when agenda a advances. Forced - When doom would be placed on or moved to a scenario card outside of the 'place doom' step of the mythos phase: place the doom on this agenda instead.

cards.flavor: With the black knight behind you and Merlin in front of you, you really are caught between a rock and a hard place.
cards.name: The Legendary Magician
cards.text: Doom cannot be placed on this agenda during the 'place doom' step of the mythos phase. Doom on this agenda does not count towards the doom threshold of agenda a and is not removed when agenda a advances. Forced - When doom would be placed on or moved to a scenario card outside of the 'place doom' step of the mythos phase: place the doom on this agenda instead.

cards.flavor:
There is no end of attractions for the average attendee to visit. And even for the less average there is plenty to be learnt if you know where to look.
Any information you gather now will certainly help you in your confrontation with Merlin, but any time you spend may help him just as much.
cards.name: All the Fun of the Fair
cards.text: [reaction] When a story card effect would allow you to move clues you control to a story card, spend up to X resources, where X is the total number of clues: Move X clues from your location to the story card instead. Objective - When The Order Pavilion is revealed, the investigators must immediately advance.

cards.name: A Meeting, Preordained
cards.text:
You force your way through to the access restricted section on the second floor of the pavilion, a plan rapidly formulating in your mind.
Remove each story card attached to a location from the game. Read Scenario Interlude: Merlin's Goals in the Campaign Guide.

cards.flavor: You don't know how long Merlin has been preparing for this moment, but it's almost certainly more than the half day you've put in to your plan. Still, the best laid plans of mice and men often go awry.
cards.name: Best Laid Plans
cards.text: Objective - Find and complete an objective on another scenario card.

cards.name: An Impossible Outcome
cards.text:
Merlin is known for his ability to see into the future but such a thing only allows him to see things that are possible.
Looking at the back of this card is an impossible action, outside the scope of Merlin's foresight. Deal 1[per_investigator] damage to Merlin or remove 1[per_investigator] doom from agenda c.

cards.name: British Government Pavilion
cards.text: If you fail a skill test printed on an attached story card remember that you 'caused trouble with the police.' Haunted - Until the end of the round, if an investigator performs an action or reaction printed on an attached story card remember that they 'caused trouble with the police.'

cards.flavor: A range of government departments have contributed exhibits, with topics ranging from the abolition of tropical disease to weather reporting. The pavilion is also home to the impressive Admiralty Theatre, showing The Defences of London, a performance touting the merits of a standing air force.
cards.name: British Government Pavilion
Burmese Pavilion
cards.text: If you fail a skill test printed on an attached story card, resolve the haunted effect on a different [[Attraction]] location as if it were at this location. Haunted - Resolve the haunted effect on a different [[Attraction]] location as if it were at this location.

cards.flavor: The fan favourite display is the dairy produce section's butter sculpture of the Prince of Wales standing beside his horse outside his ranch at Pekisko, Alberta.

cards.flavor: Covering six acres, the pavilion contains displays on mineral mining, including of precious metals; sections on farming, the dairy industry and forestry; exhibits on Canadian industry; a section on hydro-electric power and displays advertising Canada as a holiday destination, including a working model of Niagara Falls.

cards.flavor: Constructed in exactly 300 days at the sizable sum of £750,000 the stadium was completed only four days before it's inaugural event, the 1923 FA Cup Final. The stadium will be demolished after the exhibition.
cards.name: Empire Stadium

cards.flavor: You are surrounded by happy faces. Happy, ignorant faces.

cards.flavor: The hustle and bustle of crowds lightly jostle you about. Everyone is excited to experience the wonders the exhibition has to offer, unaware of what is happening behind the scenes.

cards.flavor: The India Pavilion was modelled on the Jama Masjid in Delhi and the Taj Mahal in Agra and contains 27 courts displaying different products as well an exhibition of Indian art. The organisation of these displays have led to calls for a boycott of the exhibition from Indian nationalists.

cards.flavor: The pavilion for the Order of the Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table contains a number of exhibits dedicated to the life of King Arthur, including artifacts supposedly retrieved by the recent Miskatonic expedition.

cards.flavor: Amongst the exhibits is a newly constructed steam locomotive, the flagship of the London and North Eastern Railway company: the Flying Scotsman.

cards.name: The Fortune Teller
cards.text:
A short distance from the entry gate a hooded figure sits behind a woolen blanket laid out along the side of the path. The other visitors give them a wide berth and it is apparent this is not a sanctioned attraction. It is, however, a familiar sight to you, as it would be to anyone who has walked through Arkham's Independence Square. The blanket, and the four cards rested upon it, are identical to the ones owned by the soothsayer and tarot reader Anna Kaslow.
Attached location gains '[free]: Approach the fortune teller. Flip The Fortune Teller and read the text on the other side.'

cards.name: Truth of the Cards
cards.text:
The fortune teller reveals the first of the four cards as you approach: The Ace of Cups. 'One represents location.' She begins, 'And a way forward. Enough for some to form a solution, but little more.' She reveals the second card: Three of Pentacles. 'Three represents the means. A factor that helps and harms all in equal measure. The third card: Five of Swords. 'Five represents an answer to prior actions, and a possible defence against future ones.' She pauses for a moment before revealing the final card. 'Most...would be satisfied with five.' The fourth card: Eight of Wands. 'Eight represents motive, indiscernible. A path only for those in pursuit of flawless victory.' Once you are confident she has nothing left to say you turn away, pondering the meaning behind the specific number. A wry grin begins to spread across the fortune teller's face, 'The answer lies in the cards.' You turn back in response but the woman, the blanket, and the cards are gone.
Add this card to the victory display.

cards.name: Police vs Knights vs Cultists
The Inspector Returns
cards.text:
It was supposed to be a simple task. Head down to Tintagel, interview a few members of the Order Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table, then close off any of the incident reports from the British Empire Exhibition. The inspector sighs and puts through a telegram. Then he grabs his coat and heads out onto the streets to put an end to this disorder.
Spawn the set aside Inspector Harris enemy at the Police Station. If the investigators injured Inspector Harris in London, deal 1 damage to Inspector Harris. If the investigators injured Inspector Harris at the exhibition, deal 1 damage to Inspector Harris.

cards.flavor: The appearance of a Metropolitan Police inspector may have gone some way to improve the spirits of the beleaguered officers. It has also had little impact in helping them regain control, though you have heard through the grapevine that the inspector has prepared more extreme measures.

cards.flavor: The roar of an engine blares out, followed by the sound of gunfire. The knights are unsurprisingly not prepared to handle an armoured car and the cultist aren't faring much better. You'd rather like to have the situation under control before you can find out how things could escalate further.

Calling in the Big Guns
cards.text:
The armoured car was only the beginning. As night begins to set in on the small English village the full force of the British army falls upon Tintagel.
3

cards.name: The Knight Falls
cards.text:
The King collapses before you, he and his troops slowly dissipating into a thin black mist. You feel a weight you didn't know you were carrying lift from your shoulders. You could walk away now, the threat finally gone. But you are only one step away from Avalon, and even though you cannot see her you can feel Morgan Le Fey staring through your very soul.
5

cards.flavor: If you can't deal with this Ancient One with the classic Arkham investigative method there's always the classic American method of filling it full of holes.
cards.name: Another Day in the Office
cards.text: Objective - When The Black Knight is defeated, advance.

cards.name: Behind The Scenes
cards.text:
The knight collapses before you, its body slowly dissipating into a thin black mist. You feel a weight you didn't know you were carrying lift from your shoulders. You could walk away now, the threat finally gone. But you are only one step away from Avalon, and even though you cannot see her you can feel Morgan Le Fey staring through your very soul.
5

cards.flavor: While you would rather not step into Arkham Asylum, be it real or an illusion, you have no other option if you want to be freed of the Black Knight both mentally and physically.
cards.name: Homecoming
cards.text: Forced - At the end of the round: Each investigator at The Black Knight's location takes 1 horror. Objective - When all investigators are at Arkham Asylum, advance.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text:
You walk through the quiet halls of the asylum, your footsteps echoing. You eyes are drawn to the patient admissions left carelessly at reception. Glancing through the documents you quickly notice the only name written there is yours. You are drawn away from the papers by the sound of clanking of armour, echoing at you from all directions. You turn slowly, and are faced with multiple knights clad in black. You pull yourself together and prepare to fight, otherwise those admission forms will become more than just a false reality.
Flip The Black Knight. Gather the set aside Black Knight enemies and spawn them at the following locations: Knight's Folly - King Arthur's Arms. The Savage - Police Station. Disloyal Knight - High Street. Lord of War - Village Green. Advance to Act 4a: Mind Against Matter. Put this card next to the act deck for reference.

cards.flavor: You will destroy as many of these illusions as you have to.
cards.name: Mind Against Matter
cards.text: Forced - When The Black Knight would be defeated, if there is another copy of The Black Knight in play: Remove a random other copy of The Black Knight from the game. Then, heal all damage from The Black Knight and move it to the removed copy's location. Forced - At the end of the round: Each investigator at The Black Knight's location takes 1 horror. Objective - When The Black Knight is defeated, advance.

cards.name: The Knight Falls
cards.text:
The final knight collapses before you, his body slowly dissipating into a thin black mist. You feel a weight you didn't know you were carrying lift from your shoulders. You could walk away now, the threat finally gone. But you are only one step away from Avalon, and even though you cannot see her you can feel Morgan Le Fey staring through your very soul.
5

cards.flavor: The appearance of the red knight is surely more than good fortune. It is most likely tied to the identity of the Black Knight, to Sir Balin. You only need to allow the two to meet.
cards.name: The Red Knight
cards.text: Objective - When The Guarding Knight moves to The Lake, advance.

cards.name: Sir Balin
cards.text:
As soon as the two knights catch sight of each other their weapons are drawn and the two charge at each other. Oblivious of their surroundings the two clash and will surely not stop until one, or both, are dead.
Reveal The Lake. Flip The Black Knight and The Guarding Knight. Advance to Act 4a: He Ain't Heavy

cards.flavor: The two exchange blows, each unaware of the other's identity. The Lady of the Lake, beheaded by a sword cursed to make its bearer slay their greatest friend and lead them to destruction, will have her vengeance.
cards.name: He Ain't Heavy
cards.text: Objective - When Sir Balin the Savage and Sir Balan are defeated, advance.

cards.name: The Knight Falls
cards.text:
The two knights collapse before you, the body of Sir Balin slowly dissipating into a thin black mist. You feel a weight you didn't know you were carrying lift from your shoulders. You could walk away now, the threat finally gone. But you are only one step away from Avalon, and even though you cannot see her you can feel Morgan Le Fey staring through your very soul.
5

cards.flavor: A knight lives to siege castles, as long as you can distract their attention away from you for long enough.
cards.name: The Siege of Joyous Gard
cards.text: [free] Spend 1 clue: Exhaust a [[Knight]] enemy at your location. [action] Spend 1 clue: Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Knight]] enemy and spawn it at High Street. Shuffle the encounter deck. Objective - When there are at least 2 + 1[per_investigator] [[Knight]] cards at Village Green the assault may begin, and the investigators may choose to advance.

cards.name: Behind The Scenes
cards.text:
The assaulting knights give a guttural roar and the gates break down in the face of their charge. The battle is joined in earnest.
Discard each [[Knight]] card at Village Green. For each card discarded by this effect, deal 1 damage to a [[Knight]] enemy at Joyous Gard. Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck. Reveal Joyous Gard, Advance to Act 4a: Lancelot's Folly.

cards.flavor: If this day were to follow the legends the siege would fail and Lancelot escape. If you are to reach Avalon you have no choice but to strike him down here.
cards.name: Lancelot's Folly
cards.text: Objective - When Sir Lancelot is defeated, advance.

cards.name: The Knight Falls
cards.text:
Lancelot collapses before you, his body slowly dissipating into a thin black mist. You feel a weight you didn't know you were carrying lift from your shoulders. You could walk away now, the threat finally gone. But you are only one step away from Avalon, and even though you cannot see her you can feel Morgan Le Fey staring through your very soul.
5

cards.flavor: The shop owners are just as willing to sell you information as they are their conventional wares.
cards.name: High Street
cards.text: [free]Spend 1[per_investigator] resources: Add 1[per_investigator] clues to High Street from the token bank. If it is not act 1, gain a clue from the token bank. Haunted - Remove 1 clue from High Street.

cards.flavor: The village high street has a selection of necessary shops, including the local butcher, a post office and, by far the most popular location at current, the pharmacist.
cards.name: High Street

cards.flavor: You have assured your companions that investigating the pub is of the utmost priority.
cards.name: King Arthur's Arms
cards.text: Forced - When an investigator or enemy deals damage: They get thrown out the pub, disengage them from any enemies or investigators and move them to a connecting location. Haunted - Lose 2 resources.

cards.flavor: This quintessential example of a village pub has served the locals since the 14th century, and you since about a month ago.
cards.name: King Arthur's Arms
King Arthur's Great Halls
cards.text: [action] If there are no clues on King Arthur's Great Halls and it has a story asset attached: Take control of that asset. It is now considered part of your deck. Haunted - Place 1 of your clues on King Arthur's Great Halls.
cards.traits: Tintagel. Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table.

cards.flavor: Constructed on the site of the former Town Hall and Market Hall in Fore Street, this Masonic Hall serves as the headquarters of the Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table. Designed solely for this purpose, it features 72 stained-glass windows depicting the story of King Arthur.
cards.name: King Arthur's Great Halls
cards.text: The way to the Order's lodge is blocked by an arcane energy. You cannot move into King Arthur's Great Halls.
cards.traits: Tintagel. Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table.

cards.flavor: The most activity the station has seen since its construction.
cards.name: Police Station
cards.text: [[Police]] enemies at this location get +1 fight and +2 evade. Haunted - If you have 'caused trouble with the police', take 1 damage.

cards.flavor: Tintagel's police station usually only deals with petty crime and regular troublemakers. The handful of officers stationed here are in no way prepared for what is currently happening.
cards.name: Police Station

cards.flavor: God save Donald Duck, vaudeville and variety.
cards.name: Village Green
cards.text: You cannot discover clues from Village Green while there is a ready enemy at this location. Haunted - Move a ready unengaged non-[[Elite]] enemy at an adjacent location to this location.

cards.flavor: It would be more accurate to describe this as half a village green, as the remainder has been replaced by a supernatural phenomenon.
cards.name: Village Green

cards.flavor: A refuge for the mind, or a prison for the soul?
cards.name: Arkham Asylum
cards.text: [reaction] When you discover the last clue from this location: Heal all horror from each investigator and [[Ally]] asset at Arkham Asylum. Haunted - Take 1 horror.

cards.flavor: While it is true that the Black Knight is inextricably linked to you, an Arkham resident, you feel that perhaps the choice of building it has materialised is a little too 'on the nose'. Morgan only gives you a wry grin in response.
cards.name: Arkham Asylum
cards.text: A familiar dark energy surrounds Arkham Asylum. You cannot move into Arkham Asylum.

cards.flavor: If you had a nickel for every time a castle had materialised in a field...
cards.name: Joyous Gard
cards.text: [[Knight]] enemies at Joyous Gard cannot move out of Joyous Gard. [reaction] After you succeed at a skill test during an investigation by 2 or more: Evade a [[Knight]] enemy at this location (Group limit once per round). Haunted - Ready a [[Knight]] enemy at this location.

cards.flavor: Lancelot's personal castle, captured from the forces of evil. This formidable fortress would later become his stronghold against Arthur and Gawain's siege following the king sentencing him to death.
cards.name: Joyous Gard
cards.text: A familiar dark energy surrounds Joyous Gard. You cannot move into Joyous Gard.

cards.flavor: Balin may have no direct connection to the lake, but the lady he beheaded does. She may have lost her corporeal form but her hatred toward the knight with two swords remains.
cards.name: The Lake
cards.text: [action] Spend up to 3[per_investigator] clues: Parley. Deal X damage to Sir Balin, where X is the amount of clues spent (Group limit twice per game). Haunted - Place one of your clues on The Lake.

cards.flavor: A lake in a village green may not look all that out of place, but this is a sight you have seen once before. It's certainly a lot quieter without a certain someone around though.
cards.name: The Lake
cards.text: A familiar dark energy surrounds The Lake. You cannot move into The Lake.

cards.name: The Spring
cards.text: [action]: Test any skill(8). Add your [willpower] value to your skill value for this test. If you succeed, remove Supernatural Storm from the game. [action] If The Black Knight has no attached cards, spend up to 1[per_investigator] clues: Fight. Add your [willpower] value to your skill value for this attack. This attack deals +X damage, where X is the amount of clues spent. This action can only be used against The Black Knight. Haunted - Each investigator at The Spring gets -1[willpower] until the end of the round.

cards.flavor: This mystical spring supposedly has the ability to summon storms. It also supposedly has the ability to summon a Black Knight. The last individual to try this, Sir Calogrenant, was defeated by the knight but was later avenged by Sir Owain.
cards.name: The Spring
cards.text: A familiar dark energy surrounds The Spring. You cannot move into The Spring.

cards.flavor: A true king leads from the front. An effective king leads from the rear.
cards.name: War Camp
cards.text: Reduce the shroud of this location by 2 for each copy of Knight Contingent in play. Haunted - Ready the King with the Hundred Knights.

cards.flavor: Large scale warfare requires large scale logistics to match it. The sheer size of this camp is at odds with the space that contains it, but that is hardly the least sensical thing you've encountered on this journey.
cards.name: War Camp
cards.text: A familiar dark energy surrounds War Camp. You cannot move into War Camp.

cards.name: Sir Gawain
cards.text: Each [[Knight]] enemy at Village Green gains Aloof. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Deal 1 damage to Sir Gawain for each ready non-[[Knight]] enemy at his location. Forced - When Sir Gawain would be defeated: Add him to the victory display. Forced - When Sir Gawain would be discarded: Remove him from the game.
cards.traits: Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table.

cards.name: Sir Owain
cards.text: Sir Owain may be assigned damage dealt to any investigator at his location from Supernatural Storm. [free] Exhaust Sir Owain: Move Sir Owain to a connecting location. Any investigator at Sir Owain's location may activate this ability. Forced - When Sir Owain is defeated: Add him to the victory display.
cards.traits: Arthurian. Knight. Of The Round Table.

cards.name: Knight Contingent
cards.text: Knight Contingent cannot leave play except by it's forced effect. [action]: Test [combat](0). Any investigator at attached location may spend any number of clues, for each clue spent you get +2 for this skill test. For each point you succeed by, place 1 damage on this card (to a maximum total of 6[per_investigator]). Forced - When a [[Knight]] enemy at attached location is defeated: Place 1 damage on Knight Contingent. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, place 1 damage on Knight Contingent for each ready non-[[Knight]] enemy at attached location (to a maximum total of 6[per_investigator]). Forced - When there is 6[per_investigator] damage on this card: Move that damage to the King with the Hundred Knights. Remove this card from the game.

cards.flavor: The storm seems to follow the Black Knight. Why would anyone want to summon this in the first place?
cards.name: Supernatural Storm
cards.text: Supernatural Storm cannot leave play except by the action on The Spring. Forced - After you end your turn at Supernatural Storm's location: Take 1 damage. Forced - When the game ends: Add Supernatural Storm to the victory display.

cards.flavor: There is no shortage of young squires who seek the same glories as their masters.
cards.name: Aspirant Knight
cards.text: The first Aspirant Knight each investigator draws each round gains Surge. Aspirant Knight gets +1 fight and +1 evade for each other [[Knight]] enemy at it's location. [action] Spend 1 clue: Parley. Move Aspirant Knight to a connecting [[Tintagel]] location.

cards.name: Charles Hershaw
cards.subname: A Better Option than a Tree
cards.text: Aloof. Patrol (Nearest [[Cultist]] enemy). Each other [[Cultist]] enemy at Charles Hershaw's location gets +1 fight and +1 damage. Forced - When a [[Cultist]] enemy is defeated: Deal 1 damage to Charles Hershaw
cards.traits: Humanoid. Cultist. Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Merlin
cards.text: Prey - Highest [willpower] Hunter. Retaliate. Each investigator at Merlin's location gets -1[willpower]. Forced - When an enemy is defeated at Merlin's location: Ready Merlin.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Sorcerer. Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table. Cultist. Elite.

cards.flavor: 'I will not fail here again.'
cards.name: Merlin
cards.text: Prey - Highest [willpower] Hunter. Retaliate. Forced - When an enemy is defeated at Merlin's location: Ready Merlin.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Sorcerer. Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table. Cultist. Elite.

cards.name: Merlin
cards.text: Prey - Highest [willpower] Hunter. Retaliate. Each investigator at Merlin's location gets -1[willpower]. Forced - When an enemy is defeated at Merlin's location: Ready Merlin.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Sorcerer. Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table. Cultist. Arthurian. Elite.

cards.name: Merlin
cards.text: Prey - Highest [willpower] Hunter. Each investigator at Merlin's location gets -1[willpower]. Forced - After Merlin moves, before engaging: Exhaust Merlin.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Sorcerer. Fellowship of the Knights of the Round Table. Cultist. Arthurian. Elite.

cards.flavor: A curse is placed upon the knight after slaying his foe, forced to play the role of guardian until he too is struck down.
cards.name: The Guarding Knight
cards.text: You cannot parley with enemies at The Guarding Knight's location. [action] If there are no enemies at The Guarding Knight's location: Parley. Move The Guarding Knight to a connecting location. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Exhaust a non-[[Elite]] enemy at The Guarding Knight's location.

cards.name: Sir Balan
cards.text: Retaliate. Sir Balan cannot be healed or have damage removed. [reaction] After you succeed a fight action against Sir Balan by 2 or more: Automatically evade Sir Balin the Savage. Forced - When Sir Balan takes damage (except by Sir Balin the Savage's forced effect): Deal that much damage to Sir Balin the Savage.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Cursed. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: The Black Knight observes the situation in silence. It cannot be engaged, is immune to all player card effects and investigator actions, and cannot be defeated by any means.

cards.flavor:
'Here do you keep a hundred knights and squires,
Men so disordered, so debauched and bold,
That this our court, infected with their manners,
Shows like a riotous inn.'
- Shakespeare, King Lear
cards.name: King with the Hundred Knights
cards.text:
Retaliate.
King with the Hundred Knights gets +3 fight and +1 damage for each copy of Knight Contingent in play.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: The Black Knight observes the situation in silence. It cannot be engaged, is immune to all player card effects and investigator actions, and cannot be defeated by any means.

cards.name: Sir Balin the Savage
cards.text: Retaliate. Sir Balin the Savage cannot be healed or have damage removed. [reaction] After you succeed a fight action against Sir Balin the Savage by 2 or more: Automatically evade Sir Balan. Forced - When Sir Balin the Savage takes damage (except by Sir Balan's forced effect): Deal that much damage to Sir Balan.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Cursed. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: The Black Knight observes the situation in silence. It cannot be engaged, is immune to all player card effects and investigator actions, and cannot be defeated by any means.

cards.flavor:
"You could not give up a human heart as you could give up drinking. The drink was yours, and you could give it up: but your lover's soul was not you own: it was not at your disposal; you had a duty towards it.”
- T.H. White, The Once and Future King
cards.name: Sir Lancelot
cards.text: Sir Lancelot cannot be damaged while there is another [[Knight]] enemy at his location.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: The Black Knight observes the situation in silence. It cannot be engaged, is immune to all player card effects and investigator actions, and cannot be defeated by any means.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: Hunter. While Supernatural Storm is attached to The Black Knight, it gains +1 fight and +1 horror. Forced - When an investigator card is assigned damage from Supernatural Storm: Heal 1 damage from The Black Knight.

cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: The Black Knight observes the situation in silence. It cannot be engaged, is immune to all player card effects and investigator actions, and cannot be defeated by any means.

cards.flavor: It is time to literally face your fears.
cards.name: The Black Knight
cards.text: Massive. Forced - When you take horror at The Black Knight's location: Treat that horror as direct.

cards.name: Sir Aglovale
cards.text:
Hunter.
[reaction] When you successfully attack Sir Aglovale, if you succeeded by 2 or more: deal 1 damage to Sir Aglovale.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Sir Bedivere
cards.text:
Hunter.
[reaction] When you take any amount of horror: deal 1 damage to Sir Bedivere. Any investigator at Sir Bedivere's location may trigger this ability.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Sir Bors
cards.text: [reaction] When you take any amount of damage: deal 1 damage to Sir Bors. Any investigator at Sir Bors's location may trigger this ability.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.flavor: Arthur's court jester, knighted as a cruel joke.
cards.name: "Sir" Dagonet
cards.text:
Aloof. Patrol (Nearest [[Knight]] enemy).
Each other [[Knight]] enemy at "Sir" Dagonet's location gets +1 fight.
cards.traits: Arthurian. Knight. Of The Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Sir Galahad
cards.text: Hunter [action]: Parley. Test [willpower](3). If you succeed, deal 1 damage to Sir Galahad. If you control The Holy Grail you may remove it from the game to deal an additional X damage, where X is equal to the amount of remaining sanity on The Holy Grail.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Sir Gawain
cards.text: Hunter. [action]: Fight. You may spend any amount of resources. You get +1 skill value for each resource spent. If this attack is successful and you spent at least 2 resources, this attack deals +1 damage.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Sir Hector
cards.text: [action]: Fight. You may discard any number of cards from your hand. Gain +1 skill value for each skill icon on each card. If this attack is successful and you discarded at least 1 card, this attack deals +1 damage.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Sir Lamorak
cards.text:
Hunter.
[reaction] When you discard an encounter card: deal 1 damage to Sir Lamorak. Any investigator at Sir Lamorak's location may trigger this ability.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Sir Owain
cards.text:
Hunter.
[reaction] When you successfully evade Sir Owain, if you succeeded by 2 or more: deal 1 damage to Sir Owain.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Sir Percival
cards.text: Hunter. [action]: Parley. Test [intellect](3). If you succeed, deal 1 damage to Sir Percival.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: Sir Urien
cards.text:
Hunter.
[reaction] When you successfully attack Sir Urien, if Morgan Le Fay is at your location: You deal +1 damage.
cards.traits: Humanoid. Arthurian. Knight. Of the Round Table. Elite.

cards.name: The Once and Future King
cards.text:
Easy / Standard
[skull]: -2 (-4 instead if you are at a [[Battlefield]] location).
[cultist]: -3. If you fail and you are at a [[Battlefield]] location take 1 damage. If you fail and you are not at a [[Battlefield]] location take 1 horror.
[tablet]: -3. If you fail, add 1 damage token to the [[Battlefield]] location with the lowest AFV.
[elder_thing]: -3. If you fail, add 1 horror token to the [[Battlefield]] location with the highest MFV.

cards.name: The Once and Future King
cards.text:
Hard / Expert
[skull]: -3 (-5 instead if you are at a [[Battlefield]] location).
[cultist]: -5. If you fail and you are at a [[Battlefield]] location, take 1 damage. If you fail and you are not at a [[Battlefield]] location take 1 horror.
[tablet]: -5. If you fail, add 1 damage token to the [[Battlefield]] location with the lowest AFV.
[elder_thing]: -5. If you fail, add 1 horror token to the [[Battlefield]] location with the highest MFV.

cards.flavor: Leaving Britain in Mordred's hands while fighting Lancelot abroad, Arthur returned to find he had been declared dead and Mordred taken the throne. With only the knights under his command from his previous campaign Mordred's allies outnumber him two to one, but each of Arthur's soldiers is a proven veteran.
cards.name: The True King
cards.text: Do not add doom to this agenda during the mythos phase. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Add X doom to this agenda, where X is the total number of [[Battlefield]] locations where the AFV is higher than the MFV.

cards.name: Arthur Advances
cards.text:
Arthur's knights fight with both strength and loyalty. They cut down their enemies, defying all odds.
The lead investigator searches the encounter deck and discard pile for a copy of Marching Orders and draws it, ignoring surge. Shuffle the encounter deck. Add 1 damage to each [[Battlefield]] location.
Check campaign log. If Sir Gawain is listed under 'Knights Slain':
As Arthur rests his loyal knight Gawain comes to him in a dream. He bears a warning that engaging in this battle will result in Arthur's death. A prophecy of a fate already sealed.
Remove 1 horror from each [[Battlefield]] location.

cards.flavor: Driven by moral justice, or perhaps by your influence, Arthur's knights continue to punish Mordred's troops across the battlefield.
cards.name: Punishing Treason
cards.text: Do not add doom to this agenda during the mythos phase. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Add X doom to this agenda, where X is the total number of [[Battlefield]] locations where the AFV is higher than the MFV. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase. for each [[Battlefield]] location. where AFV is at least 5 more than MFV: Place 1 damage on that location.

cards.name: The Fall of Mordred and Arthur
cards.text:
Mordred's patience with the lack of progress has worn thin. As he heads to the centre of the battlefield to finally put an end to things he catches sight of Arthur commanding his troops. You can only watch on as he charges Arthur only to be run through by his opponent's spear once again. Once again Mordred brings his sword down upon Arthur. Once again a sudden bright flash of light blinds you.
2

cards.flavor: Despite claiming the throne through questionable means Mordred commands a great deal of respect from his followers, who believe him to be a better ruler than their former king.
cards.name: The Usurper
cards.text: Do not add doom to this agenda during the mythos phase. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Add X doom to this agenda, where X is the total number of [[Battlefield]] locations where the MFV is higher than the AFV.

cards.name: Mordred Advances
cards.text:
The sizable numbers of Mordred's army is overwhelming for Arthur's knights. Even if they manage to best the odds it would be a phyrric victory at best.
The lead investigator searches the encounter deck and discard pile for a copy of Marching Orders and draws it, ignoring surge. Shuffle the encounter deck. Discard the non-[[Elite Arthur's Forces]] with the highest fight at the location with the highest MFV.
If Sir Gawain is in play and at a [[Battlefield]] location:
Only a handful of knights will survive this battle. For Gawain his fate was sealed far before your arrival.
Place horror tokens equal to Sir Gawain's remaining health on his location. Sir Gawain is defeated.

cards.flavor: Mordred's army is well on the way to seizing control of the country by sheer military might.
cards.name: A New Rule
cards.text: Do not add doom to this agenda during the mythos phase. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Add X doom to this agenda, where X is the total number of [[Battlefield]] locations where the MFV is higher than the AFV. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase, for each [[Battlefield]] location where MFV is at least 5 more than AFV: Place 1 horror on that location.

cards.name: The Fall of Arthur and Mordred
cards.text:
Mordred's advance is seemingly unstoppable and in response Arthur himself has forced his way through to the centre of the battlefield. You can only watch on as he charges Mordred through with his spear once again. Once again Mordred brings his sword down upon Arthur. Once again a sudden bright flash of light blinds you.
2

cards.flavor: Is this really the Avalon you worked so hard to reach? The once glorious king, and now you, trapped to forever repeat his final moments? If anyone is to escape this cruel fate, you must tip the balance of the two opposing forces in such a way that it doesn't reach the inevitable conclusion.
cards.name: The Battle of Camlann
cards.text: Objective - Choose a side (You will be instructed when to advance).

cards.name: Your Final Choice
cards.text:
'Well now.' Morgan Le Fey is once again stood behind you. 'So this is the final choice you decided on for this tale. I hope you don't regret it.' Even out of your sight you are certain that she wears a wry grin.
The lead investigator searches the encounter deck and discard pile for a copy of Marching Orders and draws it, ignoring surge. Shuffle the encounter deck. Read Scenario Interlude: The Woman with the Wry Grin in the Campaign Guide.

cards.flavor: The final dice have been cast. Mordred must die so that Arthur can live.
cards.name: In the Name of the King
cards.text: Investigators cannot spend clues. Mordred cannot leave Mordred's Stronghold. You cannot move while engaged with Mordred. Objective - When Mordred is defeated, advance.

cards.name: A New Ending
cards.text:
Mordred stands alone, his fortress up in flames. He stares at you through his helmet. 'I will not regret my choices. Will you be able to say the same?' There he draws his final breath, still standing in opposition to you. Arthur has lived through this rebellion, and any reason for him to reach Avalon has been lost.
3
If Oliver Buckley saw Avalon and the investigators disturbed the flow of the legend.
Is this it? Is this really the Avalon that Oliver saw?
You may read the Your Own Ending story card instead of reaching the above resolution.

cards.flavor: The final dice have been cast. The legend of King Arthur ends with his death.
cards.name: Le Morte D'Arthur
cards.text: Investigators cannot spend clues. King Arthur cannot leave Arthur's Encampment. You cannot move while engaged with King Arthur. Objective - When King Arthur is defeated, advance.

cards.name: A New Ending
cards.text:
The king collapses before you. Panic spreads through the surrounding troops and before long Mordred and his army have seized upon this opportunity and rout the remaining knights. Nobody remains to escort their king. Arthur will never reach Avalon.
3

Siding with Arthur
cards.text:
You receive a surprisingly warm welcome within Arthur's camp and you are able to meet the king with little difficulty. Entering into the largest tent, Arthur stands at the end of a table covered in notes and maps alongside several other important looking figures. 'So, you must be the knight-errants this time around, let's hear what your plan here is.' You are almost taken aback by how easily he trusts you and he clearly notices. 'We will take any opportunity to change circumstances, whether for better or for worse. If things don't work out, then we can just try again from the beginning, isn't that right?' That last remark you quickly realise was directed behind you.
Remember that the investigators 'sided with Arthur.' Flip this card over and advance to act 1b.

cards.flavor: Just the man you were looking for.

cards.subname: The Legend, The Myth, The Man

cards.name: Siding with Mordred
cards.text:
The helmet masks Mordred's expression as he sits opposite you at the meeting table. Getting this meeting was easier than you expected but persuading the man in question may not come so simply. 'All I need to hear from you is what you have intend to do to ensure I win this war.' You slightly taken aback. You came prepared to make your case for assistance not to present a fully formed plan of action. 'You don't know, do you? They never do. But I'm sure you have a plan in mind, don't you...aunt.'
Remember that the investigators 'sided with Mordred.' Flip this card over and advance to act 1b.

cards.name: Your Own Ending
cards.text:
You've come this far and you have no intention of stopping here. You've already altered circumstances to meet your own ends what's to stop you from creating your own Avalon. 'A fascinating suggestion.' A voice you never thought you'd hear again calls to you from a myriad of multicoloured butterflies that now hover around you. 'Break something apart in order to put it back together correctly.' The butterflies seem to sense your hesitation. 'Oh, you don't need to worry about me. How many scholars get to witness the mythical Avalon firsthand. All you need to do is escort Arthur there. If you do feel bad why not bring something back for my old colleagues back at the archeology department.' With that final remark you are surrounded by a blinding light. The world around you warps and changes. Ahead of you lies an empty battlefield, dominated by a giant figure letting out an agonising scream. The true face of the king that must be brought to Avalon. But the original circumstances must be met. Excalibur must be returned to the lake and, of course, Arthur must be near death.
Flip this card.

cards.name: Her Final Role
cards.text:
'Well now, these are certainly some interesting circumstances you find yourself in.' The ever familiar voice of Morgan Le Fey rings out behind you. 'But there is always room for improvisation within a script.' You think back on what she had told you when you fist met and can only wonder if this is the outcome she had expected all along. She gives you a wry grin, as if reading your mind.
If Morgan Le Fey is part of an investigator's deck: Search that investigator's hand, deck, play area and discard for Morgan Le Fey and Duality and remove them from the game. Shuffle that investigator's deck. Then, flip this card and put it into play at The Black Boat.
Otherwise:
'Now, now. There's no need to look at me like that. That was only the role you asked me to perform, and now you have changed that role again. Perhaps for the final time.'
If Morgan Le Fey is in the victory display, remove her from the game. Then, flip this card and put it into play at The Black Boat.
